text: On the same day, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy awarded Colonel Volodymyr Baranyuk and Major Denys Prokopenko, leaders of the defense in Mariupol, the honor of Hero of Ukraine, the country's highest military award.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: During this time, while attempting to transport the killed and wounded to the hospital at Azovstal, Major Mykyta Nadtochii, commander of the Azov Regiment's second battalion, was wounded in a Russian airstrike.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 20 March, the city council of Mariupol claimed Russian forces had forcefully deported "several thousand" people to camps and remote cities in Russia over the past week.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The same day, an art school building, which had sheltered some 400 people, was destroyed in a Russian bombing.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The Mariupol City Council made the announcement through the instant messaging service Telegram, highlighting that many of those sheltering in the school were women, children and elderly.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: However, Petro Andryushchenko, an advisor to the Mayor of Mariupol, raised the concern that there was no exact number on how many people were using the school as a refuge.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: An order by Russia's Ministry of Defence to surrender, lay down arms and evacuate the city was submitted on 20 March, requesting a written response by 02:00 UTC the next day.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The ultimatum was rejected by the Ukrainian government and the mayor of Mariupol.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: By this point, one of the Ukrainian battalion commanders in the city described "bombs falling every 10 minutes".
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 21 March, the first helicopter evacuation from Azovstal took place as eight or nine seriously wounded soldiers were evacuated, including the wounded Major Nadtochii.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The four special forces members on board the second helicopter were killed, along with the helicopter's crew.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: In contrast, Ukrainian president Zelenskyy stated 90 percent of helicopter pilots sent to Mariupol during the course of the siege to resupply Ukrainian forces and evacuate the wounded were lost due to Russian air defenses.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: According to Russia, one Ukrainian Mil Mi-8 helicopter was shot down on 28 March, as it was heading to Mariupol to evacuate the leaders of the Azov Regiment.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Russia reported its forces shot down two more Ukrainian Mi-8s on 5 April, as they were once again attempting to evacuate Azov commanders.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 23 March, local authorities, including the mayor, left the city due to the deteriorating situation.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The following day, Russian forces entered central Mariupol, seizing the Orthodox Church of the Intercession of the Mother of God.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The city administration alleged that Russians were trying to demoralize residents by publicly shouting claims of Russian victories, including statements that Odesa had been captured.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Vadym Boychenko said on 27 March that while Mariupol was still under Ukrainian control, Russian forces had entered deep into the city and that the city's population needed a "complete evacuation".
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: By this point, Ukrainian soldiers had run out of food and clean drinking water, and an analyst believed that Ukrainian forces would not be able to fight on beyond a few days.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: However, Ukrainian officers refused to evacuate from the city, as they did not want to abandon their wounded and dead soldiers and civilians.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The Ukrainian government estimated that "from 20,000 to 30,000" Mariupol residents had been forcibly sent to camps in Russia under Russian military control.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: During the day, Russian forces seized the administrative building in the northern Kalmiuskyi District and the military headquarters of the Azov Regiment.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The next day, Russian forces were reported to have likely divided Ukrainian troops in the city into two and possibly even three pockets.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 2 April, Russian forces captured the SBU building in central Mariupol, after which there was no more reported fighting in the area.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 4 April, one Ukrainian battalion surrendered, with Russian officials stating two days later they captured 267 Ukrainian marines from the 503rd Battalion of the Ukrainian Naval Forces.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Due to the surrender, the lines between the Ukrainian 36th Separate Marine Brigade and the Azov Regiment had been broken.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 7 April, the DPR announced central Mariupol had been cleared of Ukrainian forces.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Meanwhile, Russian troops started an advance from the southwest on 1 April, leaving the Ukrainian military in partial control of the area around the port in the southwest of Mariupol by 7 April.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 4 April, a Russian Navy missile hit a Malta-based Dominica-flagged cargo ship, setting it on fire.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 7 April, Russian forces captured a bridge leading to the Azovstal steel plant.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The following day, Russian troops seized the southern part of Mariupol's port.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 10 April, Russian forces captured the fishing port, separating Ukrainian troops in the port from those in the Azovstal steel plant into two pockets, while a possible third pocket was centered on the Illich steel plant to the north.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The next day, DPR forces claimed to have captured 80% of Mariupol.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: During the night between 11 and 12 April, Baranyuk led the 36th Separate Marine Brigade in an attempt to break out of the Russian encirclement at the Illich steel plant to the north.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: After being spotted they broke into smaller groups, some managing to link up with fighters of the Azov Regiment at the Azovstal plant to the southeast.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Many Ukrainian servicemen were killed or captured during the breakout.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Later, the DPR claimed that they had identified the body of Baranyuk after their special forces blocked the Ukrainian breakout.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: However, on 8 May, Baranyuk appeared alive in an interview with RT, along with the 36th Brigade's Chief of Staff Dmytro Kormiankov.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: They were reported to have been captured during the breakout attempt.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Around the same time at 11 April, a 16-man detachment from a battalion of tankers of the 17th Tank Brigade, which were doing operations supporting the 36th Brigade, did not follow Baranyuk's plan and instead broke through the siege.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: They used two tanks, anti-aircraft guns and cars for transport, and after breaking out they proceeded on foot for 175 km until reaching friendly Ukrainian positions.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: For leading his men to safety, the unit's commander, Lieutenant Colonel Oleg Grudzevych, was awarded a Hero of Ukraine medal.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 12 April, Aiden Aslin, a British man fighting with the Ukrainian Marines, reported that his unit was going to surrender since they had run out of ammunition, food and other supplies.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Subsequently, in the evening, Russia stated that 1,026 marines of the 36th Separate Marine Brigade had surrendered at the Illich steel plant, including 162 officers and 400 wounded fighters.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Later, Russia said it captured an additional 134 Ukrainian servicemen, bringing the total number of prisoners to 1,160.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Ukraine confirmed nearly 1,000 Marines had been captured, including wounded and those who remained at the Illich plant.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 13 April, Russian forces secured the Illich plant, reducing the number of pockets in Mariupol to two, while Russia also announced it had taken full control of Mariupol's commercial port, which was confirmed three days later.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The commander of the Azov Regiment, Prokopenko, criticized the servicemen that had surrendered, while praising those that managed to link up with his unit.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Prokopenko, as well as Ukrainian intelligence officer Illia Samoilenko, also blamed Baranyuk for the large losses inflicted on Ukrainian forces, stating his actions were uncoordinated.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: He also said that "the situation is critical and the fighting is fierce" but that sending reinforcements and breaking the siege "can be done and it must be done as soon as possible".
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On the same day, Ukrainian Defence Ministry spokesman Oleksandr Motuzianyk reported Russia started using Tu-22M3 long-range bombers to strike targets in Mariupol.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Additionally, the complex contained a system of underground tunnels, which would make clearing the entire complex challenging.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: During the day, Russian forces captured the base of the Ukrainian National Guard's 12th Operational Brigade (Ukraine), in western Mariupol.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 16 April, DPR troops seized a police station near Mariupol's beach and Russian forces were confirmed to have seized the Vessel Traffic Control Center at the port.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: According to the officer, the Ukrainian forces from the Azovstal pocket made an armoured breakthrough to the port and provided covering fire, as the 500 besieged soldiers retreated to the Azovstal plant.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Subsequently, Russia announced all urban areas of the city had been cleared, claiming that Ukrainian forces only remained at the Azovstal Steel Plant.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: However, fighting was reported to be continuing near Flotskaya street in the western Primorsky District.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 18 April, it was estimated that 95% of the city had been destroyed in the fighting.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Ukrainian soldiers ignored a Russian ultimatum to surrender, deciding to fight to the end.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Russia threatened to "destroy" those who continued to fight on.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: That same day, Ukraine accused Russia of bombing a hospital sheltering 300 people.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Serhiy Taruta, the former governor of the Donetsk region and a Mariupol native, said 300 people, including wounded troops and civilians with children, were sheltered at the hospital.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 21 April, Russian President Vladimir Putin ordered Russian troops not to storm the Azovstal steel plant, but to blockade it instead until the Ukrainian forces there ran out of supplies.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: In his opinion, defeating Ukrainian forces at the plant would have been "really difficult" for Russian troops without an "enormous cost to both sides".
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Despite the ordered blockade, Russian forces advanced within 20 metres (66 ft) of some of the Ukrainian positions.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 22 April, the western Primorsky District was thought to be cleared by Russian forces, with no more reports of fighting, with all of the remaining Ukrainian forces surrounded in the Azovstal Steel Plant.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Hundreds of civilians had also taken refuge at the plant.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 23 April, according to Ukraine, airstrikes and an apparent ground assault recommenced on the Azovstal steel works.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: An advisor to the Ukrainian President said: "The enemy is trying to strangle the final resistance of the defenders of Mariupol in the Azovstal area".
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Ukrainian security chief Oleksiy Danilov claimed that at night, a helicopter had resupplied Azovstal.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On the same day, it was reported that Russia was redeploying forces from Mariupol to other fronts in eastern Ukraine, with Russia reportedly redeploying 12 units from Mariupol.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On the next day, Russian forces continued bombing Ukrainian positions in the Azovstal Steel Plant, with reports that Russian forces might have been planning a renewed assault on the facility.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Meanwhile, Ukrainian supplies of ammunition were dwindling, while food and water were running dangerously low.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: During the night of 27 to 28 April, the heaviest airstrikes yet were reportedly conducted against Azovstal, with more than 50 strikes by Tu-22M3, Su-25s and Su-24s aircraft hitting the facility, according to Ukraine.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: This corridor was made after a trip by Secretary-General of the United Nations António Guterres to Moscow the previous week, where he personally brokered a deal.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 30 April 20 civilians had left the Azovstal steel plant, while Russian media claimed a number of 25.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Talks were underway to try and release the remaining 1,000 or so civilians.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 2 May, about 100 civilians were reported to have been evacuated.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Russian aircraft, according to the US Department of Defense, were using dumb bombs in Mariupol.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Russian ground forces were also reported to be pulling out of the city, possibly to reinforce their positions elsewhere in the Donbas, where Russia was carrying out a large-scale offensive.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: According to one US DOD official: "Largely the efforts around Mariupol for the Russians are now in the realm of airstrikes".
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 3 May, the Russian forces in Mariupol restarted their attacks on Azovstal.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: They began an assault on the steel plant in what have been called "difficult bloody battles".
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The following day it was reported the Russians had broken into the plant.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Ukrainian politician Davyd Arakhamia said: "Attempts to storm the plant continue for the second day.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Russian troops are already on the territory of Azovstal." On 5 May, some 300 civilians were allowed to leave due to Russia opening humanitarian corridors.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 6 May, some 500 civilians, in total, had been evacuated according to the United Nations.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The Azov Regiment reported one fighter killed and six wounded while helping evacuate civilians.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: As to their current conditions, "It feels like I've landed in a hellish reality show in which us soldiers fight for our lives and the whole world watches this interesting episode.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: It's all real." President Zelenskyy promised "we are working on evacuating our military".
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 9 May, the Donetsk People's Republic held a Victory Day parade in Mariupol.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The leader of the Republic, Denis Pushilin participated in the event.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: At the same time, a meeting took place near Mariupol involving Russian military representatives and Ukrainian commanders from Azovstal, including Major Prokopenko, who were brought to the meeting place by Russian armoured vehicles from Azovstal.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: During the meeting, the terms of the Ukrainians' surrender were agreed upon.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 10 May, Ukrainian authorities reported that over 1,000 Ukrainian soldiers, hundreds of them wounded, remained trapped inside the Azovstal steelworks.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The Institute for the Study of War noted the lack of a Russian ground offensive on 12 May, but noted that Russian forces had likely secured the M14 highway the following day.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 16 May, Alexander Khodakovsky, commander of a DPR brigade stationed near Azovstal, stated that a group of nine soldiers had come out of the plant to negotiate under a white flag.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On the same day, the Ukrainian General staff announced that the Mariupol garrison had "fulfilled its combat mission" and that "evacuation" from the Azovstal steel plant had begun.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The military said that 264 service members, 53 of them seriously wounded, had been taken by bus to areas controlled by Russian forces.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The evacuation of wounded troops was followed in the subsequent days by the surrender of the remainder of the garrison.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Ukrainian Deputy Defence Minister Hanna Maliar said: "Thanks to the defenders of Mariupol, Ukraine gained critically important time to form reserves and regroup forces and receive help from partners.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Some prominent Russian lawmakers called on the government to deny prisoner exchanges for members of the Azov Regiment.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The ICRC registered the surrendered troops as prisoners of war at the request of both sides, collecting information to contact their families.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 18 May, Russian artillery and aircraft bombed once again Azovstal's remaining defenders.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The DPR leadership claimed that the local high-ranking Ukrainian commanders had not yet surrendered.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: According to Russian sources, the last defenders surrendered on 20 May, among them Lieutenant Colonel Prokopenko, Major Volynskyi and Captain Svyatoslav Palamar, deputy commander of the Azov Regiment.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 26 May, Russia reopened the Port of Mariupol to commercial vessels following mine removal.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: In the months after taking control of the city, Russian authorities had many damaged buildings torn down, sometimes even turning out the remaining residents.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Associated Press describes this ongoing process as an effort to "eradicat[e] all vestiges of Ukraine" and to cover up "the evidence of war crimes".
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Denis Pushilin claimed that some Ukrainian holdouts had been discovered and captured in the area of the Azovstal plant.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: In an explosion at Olenivka prison on 29 July 2022, 53 Ukrainian prisoners of war from Mariupol were killed and 75 wounded.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Both Ukrainian and Russian authorities accused each other of the attack on the prison.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: However, definitive proof of fault could not be established.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 21 September 2022, 215 Ukrainian prisoners of war from Mariupol were released in a prisoner swap.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Under the agreement, Ukrainian Azov Regiment Commander Denys Prokopenko and four other top Ukrainian commanders from the Azovstal siege will be required to stay in Turkey until the end of the war.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: First Deputy Mayor Mykhailo Kohut remained in Mariupol, on Boychenko's behalf, who was responsible for the direction of communal work and city defense.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: According to the head of the patrol police of Mariupol Mykhailo Vershinin, Kohut helped with the defense of the city from Russian invaders and coordinated public utilities actions under intense shelling.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Kohut held his last meeting with public utilities on 19 March, at that time street fighting was going on in the city.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The World Health Organization warned in May 2022 of potential cholera outbreaks in occupied areas, with reports of wastewater flooding streets and sewage mixing with drinking water.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 6 June 2022, Ukrainian authorities warned that all preconditions for an outbreak were present, prompting Russian occupational authorities to impose a quarantine.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Mayor Boychenko confirmed on 11 June that an outbreak of cholera had begun, with broken sanitation systems and unrecovered corpses contributing to the spread of disease.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 26 May, the Ukrainians claimed that they had killed 250 Russian soldiers in a strike on the Azovstal Steelworks, with another 200 killed the previous week.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 29 May, the Ukrainians claimed to have again struck Mariupol, this time hitting the Yalta Health Center, with 650 Russian soldiers claimed to have been housed there.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Explosives capable of destroying the bodies were found underneath them.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The bodies would be handed over to Ukraine.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: By 12 June, Russia returned the bodies of some 220 deceased Ukrainian soldiers, all of whom had been fighting in the Azovstal steelworks, while "just as many bodies" still remained in Mariupol.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: A third of these were soldiers from the Azov unit.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Subsequently, another 145 bodies of those killed in Mariupol were returned.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Initially, the State Bureau of Investigation decided to close proceedings against 277 servicemen of the 501st Separate Naval Infantry Battalion on suspicion of desertion.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: However, they later found that the battalion's commanding officers had defected to Russia and tricked the battalion into surrendering.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: According to Russia, some 3,917 Ukrainian soldiers were captured during the siege, while Ukraine confirmed more than 3,500 soldiers, with an additional battalion, were taken prisoner.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Around 700 of these were members of the Azov Battalion.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 8 June, over 1,000 prisoners of war were transferred from the DPR to Russia.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Ukraine also claimed that the 810th Naval Infantry Brigade of Russia's Black Sea Fleet had 158 killed, 500 wounded and 70 missing by mid-April.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 11 March, the city council stated that at least 1,582 civilians had been killed during the siege, increasing that number on 13 March to 2,187 having been killed by the latter date.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 14 March, Oleksiy Arestovych, an adviser to the Ukrainian president, stated that more than 2,500 civilians had been killed in Mariupol's siege.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: However, the city council later clarified that 2,357 civilians had died.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Pyotr Andryushchenko, an adviser to the city government, however stated that the council's count was inaccurate and estimated that total number of civilians killed could be as high as 20,000.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: The New York Times reported that officials in the city had been struggling to account for how many civilians had died or gone missing during the siege.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Videos posted on Telegram showed that residents of the Cheremushky neighborhood were forced to bury corpses in a courtyard, while others had to turn a post office building into a makeshift morgue, stacking it with dead bodies.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 16 March, the Associated Press (AP) reported that it had documented that many of the dead were "children and mothers" contrary, it said, to Russian government claims that civilians had not been targeted.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 12 April, city officials reported that up to 20,000 civilians had been killed.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On the same day, the Mayor of the city reported that about 21,000 civilians had been killed.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: An updated Ukrainian death toll the following month put the number of civilians killed at at least 22,000.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: By mid-June, the United Nations stated it had confirmed the deaths of 1,348 civilians, but said the true death toll was "likely thousands higher".
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: On 29 August, President of Mariupol Television, volunteer and civil activist Mykola Osychenko said to Dnipro TV that, according to the insider information, 87,000 deaths have been currently documented in morgues in Mariupol.
title: Siege of Mariupol

text: Besides, 26,750 bodies are buried in mass graves, and many more are buried in the yards of the apartment blocks and private houses, or still under the rubble.

text: The war in Donbas, also called the Donbas war, was a phase of the Russo-Ukrainian war in the eastern Donbas region of Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: The war began in April 2014, when Russian-backed paramilitaries began an uprising against the Ukrainian government, seized territory and declared independent states.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukraine's military launched an operation against them, but failed to fully retake the territory.
title: War in Donbas

text: Covertly, Russia's military were directly involved, and the separatists were largely under Russian control.
title: War in Donbas

text: This phase ended when the war was subsumed by the full-scale Russian invasion of Ukraine in 2022.
title: War in Donbas

text: In March 2014, following Ukraine's Revolution of Dignity, Russia occupied Crimea.
title: War in Donbas

text: Anti-revolution and pro-Russian protests began in Ukraine's Donetsk and Luhansk provinces, collectively 'the Donbas'.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 12 April, a commando unit led by Russian citizen Igor 'Strelkov' Girkin crossed the border from Russia and seized Sloviansk and other settlements in the Donbas.
title: War in Donbas

text: Separatists declared the Donetsk and Luhansk republics (DPR and LPR) as independent states.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russia covertly supported them with soldiers and weaponry.
title: War in Donbas

text: It only admitted sending "military specialists", but later acknowledged the separatists as Russian combat veterans.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 15 April, Ukraine began an "Anti-Terrorist Operation" (ATO).
title: War in Donbas

text: By August 2014, Ukraine had re-taken most of its territory.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russia reacted by covertly sending troops, tanks and artillery into the Donbas, helping pro-Russian forces regain much of what they had lost.
title: War in Donbas

text: DPR leader Alexander Borodai said 50,000 Russian citizens had fought for the separatists by mid-2015, excluding the regular Russian troops that invaded.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Minsk agreement was signed in September 2014.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 16 September, Ukraine passed a law to grant this self-governance and schedule elections there for December.
title: War in Donbas

text: However, the separatists held their own elections in November, which violated the Minsk agreement.
title: War in Donbas

text: The ceasefire was also broken, and Russian-backed forces began an assault on Donetsk Airport, eventually capturing it in January 2015.
title: War in Donbas

text: A new ceasefire, Minsk II, was agreed on 12 February 2015.
title: War in Donbas

text: Immediately after, Russian forces renewed their offensive on Debaltseve and forced Ukraine's military to withdraw.
title: War in Donbas

text: Both sides fortified their position by building networks of trenches, bunkers and tunnels, resulting in static trench warfare.
title: War in Donbas

text: Donbas remained a war zone, with dozens killed monthly.
title: War in Donbas

text: OSCE observers documented military convoys crossing from Russia covertly, and by the end of 2017 had counted around 30,000 people in military gear crossing from Russia at the two border checkpoints it was allowed to monitor.
title: War in Donbas

text: All sides agreed to the "Steinmeier formula" for ending the war in October 2019.
title: War in Donbas

text: Following a ceasefire and withdrawal of foreign soldiers, elections would be held under Ukrainian law and, if the OSCE deemed these free and fair, then the Donbas territories would be given "special status" and re-integrated into Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Ukrainian side demanded control of its border and the right of internally displaced Ukrainians to return to the Donbas to vote.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Donbas separatists said they would not withdraw or disarm, refused to give up control of the border, and said they would seek integration with Russia.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Russian state continued to deny that its military was in the Donbas, insisting that elections be held while Russian and Russian-backed forces still controlled the territory and the border.
title: War in Donbas

text: During 2021, Russia's proxies stepped up their attacks as Russian forces massed near Ukraine's borders.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russia recognised the DPR and LPR as independent states on 21 February 2022 and deployed "peacekeeping" troops there.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 24 February, Russia began a full-scale invasion of Ukraine, subsuming the Donbas war into it.
title: War in Donbas

text: About 14,000 people were killed in the war: 6,500 Russian and Russian proxy forces, 4,400 Ukrainian forces, and 3,400 civilians on both sides.
title: War in Donbas

text: Most civilian casualties were in the first year.
title: War in Donbas

title: War in Donbas

title: War in Donbas

text: This approach has been acceptable to eastern Ukrainian oligarchs, who do not harbour anti-Russian feelings".
title: War in Donbas

title: War in Donbas

title: War in Donbas

title: War in Donbas

title: War in Donbas

title: War in Donbas

title: War in Donbas

text: This series of events became known as the Revolution of Dignity.
title: War in Donbas

title: War in Donbas

title: War in Donbas

text: The Internal Affairs Ministry reported that the city of Sviatohirsk, near Sloviansk, was retaken by Ukrainian troops on 23 April.
title: War in Donbas

text: In addition, the Defence Ministry said it had taken control over all points of strategic importance in the area around Kramatorsk.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 24 April, 70 to 100 insurgents armed with assault rifles and rocket launchers attacked an armoury in Artemivsk.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian troops attempted to fight off the insurgents, but were forced to retreat after many men were wounded by insurgent fire.
title: War in Donbas

text: Some 30 militants seized the police headquarters in Konstantinovka on 28 April.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 24 April, Avakov said that Ukrainian troops had captured the city administration in Mariupol, after a clash with pro-Russian demonstrators there.
title: War in Donbas

text: Despite this, a report by the BBC said that whilst it appeared that Ukrainian troops and the mayor of Mariupol did enter the building in the early morning, Ukrainian troops had abandoned it by the afternoon.
title: War in Donbas

text: Local pro-Russian activists blamed Ukrainian nationalists for the attack upon the building but said that the DPR had regained control.
title: War in Donbas

text: A representative of the Republic, Irina Voropoyeva, said, "We, the Donetsk People's Republic, still control the building.
title: War in Donbas

text: There was an attempted provocation but now it's over." The same day, Ukrainian government officials said that the Armed Forces had intended to retake the city of Sloviansk, but that an increased threat of "Russian invasion" halted these operations.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russian forces had mobilised within 10 kilometres (6+1⁄4 mi) of the Ukrainian border.
title: War in Donbas

text: By 6 May, 14 Ukrainian troops had died and 66 had been injured in the fighting.
title: War in Donbas

text: Insurgents took over the offices of the regional state television network on 27 April.
title: War in Donbas

text: After capturing the broadcasting centre, the militants began to broadcast Russian television channels.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Luhansk People's Republic (LPR) was declared on 27 April.
title: War in Donbas

text: Representatives of the Republic demanded that the Ukrainian government provide amnesty for all protesters, enshrine Russian as an official language, and hold a referendum on the status of the region.
title: War in Donbas

text: They issued an ultimatum that stated that if Kyiv did not meet their demands by 14:00 on 29 April, they would launch an insurgency in tandem with that of the Donetsk People's Republic.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 29 April, a city administration building in Pervomaisk was overrun by Luhansk People's Republic insurgents, who then raised their flag over it.
title: War in Donbas

text: In Krasnyi Luch, the city administration conceded to demands by separatist activists that it support the referendums on the status of Donetsk and Luhansk of 11 May, and followed by raising the Russian flag over the city administration building.
title: War in Donbas

text: Insurgents occupied the city administration building in Kadiivka on 1 May.
title: War in Donbas

text: Later in the week, they captured the local police station, business centre, and SBU building.
title: War in Donbas

text: The same day, the police headquarters in Slovianoserbsk was seized by members of the Army of the South-East, affiliated with the Luhansk People's Republic.
title: War in Donbas

text: The town of Antratsyt was occupied by a number of renegade Don Cossacks.
title: War in Donbas

text: Insurgents went on to seize the prosecutor's office in Sievierodonetsk on 7 May.
title: War in Donbas

text: The next day, supporters of the Luhansk People's Republic captured government buildings in Starobilsk.
title: War in Donbas

text: After a government counter-offensive as part of the "Anti-Terrorist Operation" in Donetsk Oblast on 2–3 May, the insurgents were routed from Kramatorsk's occupied SBU building.
title: War in Donbas

text: Despite this, Ukrainian troops quickly withdrew from the city for unknown reasons, and the separatists quickly regained control.
title: War in Donbas

text: Sporadic fighting continued until 5 July, when the insurgents withdrew from Kramatorsk.
title: War in Donbas

text: In Mariupol, the city administration building was briefly retaken by the Ukrainian National Guard on 7 May.
title: War in Donbas

text: The pro-Russian forces quickly took the building back.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian troops launched another attack on insurgents in Mariupol on 9 May.
title: War in Donbas

text: A police building occupied by insurgents was set alight by government forces, causing the insurgents to flee.
title: War in Donbas

text: Internal Affairs minister Avakov said that 60 insurgents attacked the police building, not Ukrainian troops and that the police and other government forces repelled the insurgents.
title: War in Donbas

text: Between six and twenty militants were killed, along with one police officer.
title: War in Donbas

text: Four militants were captured, and five policemen were wounded.
title: War in Donbas

text: During the fighting, pro-Russian protesters seized one armoured personnel carrier, and afterwards barricaded the city centre with tyres.
title: War in Donbas

text: The same day, separatists attempting to disarm Ukrainian troops near Donetsk were met with warning shots, and 100 were detained.
title: War in Donbas

text: In his decree, he demanded that all military stationed in the region swear an oath of allegiance to him within 48 hours, and said that all remaining Ukrainian military in the region would be "destroyed on the spot".
title: War in Donbas

text: He then petitioned the Russian Federation for military support to protect against "the threat of intervention by NATO" and "genocide".
title: War in Donbas

text: Pavel Gubarev, president of Donetsk People's Republic, instituted martial law on 15 May, and vowed for "total annihilation" of Ukrainian forces if they did not pull out of the Donbas by 21:00.
title: War in Donbas

text: Similarly, the president of the Luhansk People's Republic, Valery Bolotov, declared martial law on 22 May.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Donetsk-based steel magnate Rinat Akhmetov called on his 300,000 employees within the Donetsk region to "rally against separatists" on 20 May.
title: War in Donbas

text: Sirens sounded at noon at his factories to signal the beginning of the rally.
title: War in Donbas

text: A so-called "Peace March" was held in the Donbas Arena in Donetsk city, accompanied by cars sounding their horns at noon.
title: War in Donbas

text: BBC News and Ukrainska Pravda reported that some vehicles were attacked by separatists, and that gunmen had warned the offices of several city taxi services not to take part.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 16 May, Metinvest steelworkers, along with local police and security forces, routed the insurgents from the city administration and other occupied government buildings in the city.
title: War in Donbas

text: Most insurgents left the city, but some could still be seen outside the burnt city administration building.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 22 May, 18 Ukrainian soldiers were killed during an insurgent attack upon an army checkpoint near the city of Volnovakha.
title: War in Donbas

text: Three armoured personnel carriers and several lorries were destroyed in the attack, whilst one insurgent was killed.
title: War in Donbas

text: The same day, a convoy consisting of 100 soldiers attempted to cross a bridge at Rubizhne, Luhansk Oblast, and advance into insurgent-held territory.
title: War in Donbas

text: They were ambushed by a group of between 300 and 500 insurgents.
title: War in Donbas

text: After fighting that lasted throughout the day, the soldiers were forced to retreat.
title: War in Donbas

text: Between two and fourteen soldiers and between seven and twenty insurgents were killed during the fighting.
title: War in Donbas

text: Three army infantry combat vehicles and one lorry were destroyed, and another three armoured vehicles were captured by the insurgents.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Internal Affairs Ministry stated that some insurgents had attempted to enter Luhansk Oblast from Russia, but had been repelled by border guards.
title: War in Donbas

text: Following a declaration by Pavel Gubarev establishing the "New Russia Party" on 22 May, representatives of the Donetsk and Luhansk republics signed an agreement creating the confederative state of New Russia.
title: War in Donbas

text: Separatists planned to incorporate most of Ukraine's southern and eastern regions into the new confederation, including the key cities of Kharkiv, Kherson, Dnipropetrovsk, Mykolaiv, Zaporizhzhia and Odesa.
title: War in Donbas

text: The declaration signed established the position of Russian Orthodoxy as the state religion and an intention to nationalise key industries.
title: War in Donbas

text: A unit of the pro-government Donbas Battalion volunteer paramilitary attempted to advance on a separatist checkpoint near the village of Karlivka, northwest of Donetsk city, on 23 May.
title: War in Donbas

text: They were ambushed by a group of between 150 and 200 separatists, supported by one of the captured armoured personnel carriers.
title: War in Donbas

text: The pro-government paramilitary was surrounded by the separatists, and outnumbered six to one until fighters affiliated with the nationalist Right Sector broke through the separatist lines to allow some members of the group to escape.
title: War in Donbas

text: Five members of the Donbas Battalion were killed, along with four separatists.
title: War in Donbas

text: Twenty members of the pro-government paramilitaries were wounded, and at least four were captured.
title: War in Donbas

text: The involvement of Right Sector was disputed by the leadership of the Donbas Battalion.
title: War in Donbas

text: Pro-Russian leader Igor Bezler said that he executed all of the captured paramilitaries.
title: War in Donbas

text: Another separatist leader confirmed four of their fighters were killed, and also said that ten pro-government paramilitaries and two civilians died.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Ukrainian National Guard issued an ultimatum to the separatists, asking them to surrender.
title: War in Donbas

text: Government forces then launched an assault on separatist positions at the airport with paratroopers and airstrikes.
title: War in Donbas

text: An estimated 40 insurgents died in the fighting, with some civilians caught in the crossfire.
title: War in Donbas

text: Between 15 and 35 insurgents were killed in a single friendly-fire incident, when two lorries carrying wounded fighters away from the airport were ambushed by insurgents mistaking them for Ukrainian forces.
title: War in Donbas

text: During the fighting at the airport, Druzhba Arena in Donetsk city was ransacked by pro-Russian insurgents, who looted the building and destroyed surveillance equipment, and set it ablaze.
title: War in Donbas

text: Concurrently, Donetsk police said the insurgents had killed two policemen in the nearby town of Horlivka.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Moscow Times reported that the two men had been executed for "breaking their oath to the Donetsk People's Republic".
title: War in Donbas

text: Mykhailo Koval, the Minister of Defence, said on 30 May that Ukrainian government forces had "completely cleared" the insurgents from the southern and western parts of Donetsk Oblast and the northern part of Luhansk Oblast.
title: War in Donbas

text: Two days later, five separatists were killed when 500 separatists attacked a border post in Luhansk Oblast.
title: War in Donbas

text: Separatists blamed the incident on a government airstrike, while Ukrainian officials denied this, and claimed that the explosions were caused by a stray surface-to-air missile fired by insurgents.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Organisation for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE) published a report the next day, stating that based on "limited observation", they believed that the explosion was caused by an airstrike, supporting separatist claims.
title: War in Donbas

text: A CNN investigation found clear evidence that the attack came from the air and the pattern of the craters suggested use of standard equipment on the Su-25, a ground-attack fighter, and the Su-27 – both combat aircraft operated by Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: Although the Russian Federation also uses Sukhoi Su-27, it was not mentioned in the CNN news article as even a possibility.
title: War in Donbas

text: Radio Liberty also concluded that "Despite Denials, All Evidence For Deadly Explosion Points To Kyiv".
title: War in Donbas

text: CNN said that it was the first time that civilians had been killed in an attack by the Ukrainian air force during the 2014 pro-Russian unrest in the Donbas.
title: War in Donbas

text: Two soldiers were killed in the fighting, and forty-five were wounded, with conflicting reports about losses on the insurgent side.
title: War in Donbas

text: Per government sources, 300 insurgents were killed and 500 wounded, but insurgents said they lost between 10 and 50.
title: War in Donbas

text: The next day, insurgents captured the besieged Luhansk border post (seizing vast quantities of munitions) and a National Guard base near Luhansk city.
title: War in Donbas

text: The fighting in these areas left six insurgents dead, and three government soldiers wounded.
title: War in Donbas

text: Another border post was captured by the insurgents in Sverdlovsk.
title: War in Donbas

text: The National Guard base fell after guardsmen ran out of ammunition.
title: War in Donbas

text: Another border post was attacked on 5 June, in the village of Marynivka.
title: War in Donbas

text: Government officials said that between 15 and 16 insurgents were killed and that five soldiers were injured as well.
title: War in Donbas

text: A shootout between rival separatist groups in Donetsk city took place on 7 June, near the Donetsk RSA.
title: War in Donbas

text: Internal Affairs Minister Arsen Avakov said "we have observed columns passing with armoured personnel carriers, other armoured vehicles and artillery pieces, and tanks which, according to our information, came across the border and this morning were in Snizhne".
title: War in Donbas

text: He said Ukrainian forces had destroyed part of the column, and that fighting was still under way.
title: War in Donbas

text: Reuters correspondents confirmed the presence of three tanks in Donetsk city, and the US State Department's Bureau of Intelligence and Research also said that Russia had sent tanks, along with other heavy weapons, to the separatists in Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: The newly elected Ukrainian president Petro Poroshenko said that it was "unacceptable" for tanks to cross into Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: Nevertheless, the three tanks were later spotted moving through Makiivka and Torez, flying the flag of Russia.
title: War in Donbas

text: Insurgents confirmed that they had obtained three tanks, but leaders refused to elaborate on how they acquired them; one militant told reporters that they came "from a military warehouse".
title: War in Donbas

text: The president of the DPR, Denis Pushilin, said the three tanks would be stationed in Donetsk city and that they gave his forces "at least some hope of defending [Donetsk] because heavy weapons are already being used against us".
title: War in Donbas

text: Konstantin Mashovets, a former Ukrainian Defence Ministry official, said the tanks had likely been seized by Russian forces in Crimea before making their way into mainland Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: The next morning, a convoy of border guardsmen was attacked by insurgents while passing Mariupol, leaving at least five of the guardsmen dead.
title: War in Donbas

text: The aircraft was preparing to land at Luhansk International Airport, and was carrying troops and equipment from an undisclosed location.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian troops tried to retake Yampil with the goal of breaking through to Siversk.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to the Armed Forces, it started after insurgents attempted to break through a cordon of government troops around government-held Krasny Lyman.
title: War in Donbas

text: The battle was described as exceeding "in terms of force and scale anything there has been" during the conflict in the Donbas.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Ukrainian military deployed both air and artillery strikes in their attempts to rout the insurgents.
title: War in Donbas

text: Overnight, between 7 and 12 soldiers were killed and between 25 and 30 were wounded.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Armed Forces said they killed 300 insurgents, but this was not independently verified, the separatists confirmed only two deaths and seven wounded on their side.
title: War in Donbas

text: The insurgents also said they destroyed one tank, several BMD-1s, and also shot down a Su-25 bomber.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Ukrainian military said that they had gained control of Yampil and Siversk on 20 June.
title: War in Donbas

text: A unilateral ceasefire by Ukrainian forces, as part of Ukrainian president Petro Poroshenko's 15-point peace plan, was due to begin the next day.
title: War in Donbas

text: By this point, the number of soldiers killed in the battle had reached 13.
title: War in Donbas

text: Shelling occurred in Kramatorsk and Sloviansk, and government forces retook a border crossing in Dolzhansk, one of the three major border crossings occupied by the separatists.
title: War in Donbas

text: Government forces also recaptured the villages of Brusivka and Stary Karavan.
title: War in Donbas

text: The same day, insurgents in Luhansk said that they had taken control of Luhansk International Airport.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 1 July 2014 in Donetsk a street gunfight broke between rival factions of pro-Russian militants, which resulted in one person being fatally wounded and two others in critical conditions.
title: War in Donbas

text: Internal Affairs Ministry spokesman Zoryan Shkyriakuk said that over 1,000 pro-Russian insurgents were killed in the first day following the resumption of hostilities.
title: War in Donbas

text: Liga.net, citing a source involved with the government military operation, reported that over 400 insurgents were killed in action, but that the higher figures reported earlier could not be confirmed.
title: War in Donbas

text: Separatists themselves reported only two deaths in fighting at Mykolaivka.
title: War in Donbas

text: Insurgents attacked a border post in Novoazovsk on 2 July.
title: War in Donbas

text: During the attack, mortars were fired upon the post, and clashes broke out.
title: War in Donbas

text: One border guard was killed in the fighting, and another eight guardsmen were injured.
title: War in Donbas

text: Government forces recaptured the town of Mykolaivka, near Sloviansk, on 4 July.
title: War in Donbas

text: A group of DPR-affiliated militants defected as a result, and joined the Ukrainian army.
title: War in Donbas

text: In a further blow to the insurgents, government forces retook the stronghold of Sloviansk on 5 July.
title: War in Donbas

text: Commander of the DPR insurgents, Igor Girkin, took the decision "due to the overwhelming numerical superiority of the enemy", according to DPR prime minister Alexander Borodai.
title: War in Donbas

text: He said that DPR forces had retreated to Kramatorsk, but BBC News reported that they were seen abandoning their checkpoints in Kramatorsk.
title: War in Donbas

text: Later that day, Borodai confirmed that the insurgents had abandoned "the entire northern sector", including Kramatorsk, and had retreated to Donetsk city.
title: War in Donbas

text: After the retreat of Girkin's forces to Donetsk, he assumed control of the DPR, replacing the previous authorities there in what was described as a "coup d'état".
title: War in Donbas

text: Subsequently, Ukraine's Armed Forces recaptured Druzhkivka, Kostyantynivka, and Artemivsk.
title: War in Donbas

text: Amidst the insurgent retreat, Donetsk city mayor Oleksandr Lukyanchenko said that at least 30,000 people had left the city since April.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ahead of a planned government offensive on the insurgent-occupied city of Donetsk, key roads leading into the city were blocked on 7 July.
title: War in Donbas

text: Insurgents destroyed railway bridges over the roads, causing them to collapse and block the roads.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukraine's Defence Minister Valeriy Heletey stated on 8 July that there would be "no more unilateral ceasefires", and said dialogue was only possible if the insurgents laid down their weapons.
title: War in Donbas

text: More fighting broke out at Luhansk International Airport on 9 July.
title: War in Donbas

text: LPR-affiliated insurgents said that they had captured the airport on 1 July, but the Ukrainian army managed to maintain control over it.
title: War in Donbas

text: More than 10,000 households in Luhansk Oblast were without gas service due to damage to gas lines.
title: War in Donbas

text: Clashes at the Donetsk International Airport continued on 10 July.
title: War in Donbas

text: Insurgents fired mortars at the airport, and attempted to recapture it, but were repelled by Ukrainian troops.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian forces also retook the city of Siversk.
title: War in Donbas

text: The same day, the Luhansk city administration reported that six civilians had been injured due to ongoing hostilities across the city.
title: War in Donbas

text: There were also reports of factionalism among the separatists, with some desertions.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to these reports, the Vostok Battalion had rejected the authority of Igor Girkin.
title: War in Donbas

text: Alexander Borodai, prime minister of the DPR, denied these reports.
title: War in Donbas

text: Heavy fighting continued in Luhansk Oblast on 11 July.
title: War in Donbas

text: On that day, a Ukrainian column travelling near Rovenky was attacked by an insurgent-operated Grad rocket lorry.
title: War in Donbas

text: A Ukrainian airstrike destroyed the rocket launcher, but only after 23 soldiers were killed.
title: War in Donbas

text: In response to the attack, Ukrainian president Poroshenko said that "For every life of our soldiers, the militants will pay with tens and hundreds of their own".
title: War in Donbas

text: The next day, the Ukrainian Air Force launched airstrikes on insurgent positions across Donetsk and Luhansk oblasts.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Ukrainian government said that 500 insurgents were killed in these strikes, which they said were retaliations for the separatist rocket attack the previous day.
title: War in Donbas

text: Four people were killed at Marinka, a western suburb of Donetsk city, after rockets struck an insurgent-held area.
title: War in Donbas

text: Shells landed on the border town of Donetsk in Rostov Oblast, a part of Russia, on 13 July.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russian officials blamed the Armed Forces of Ukraine, whilst Ukraine denied responsibility and accused insurgents in the Donbas of having staged a false flag attack.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russia said it was considering launching airstrikes against Ukrainian government targets as retaliation.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian forces ended an insurgent blockade of Luhansk International Airport on 14 July.
title: War in Donbas

text: LPR officials acknowledged that they lost 30 men during fighting in the village of Oleksandrivka.
title: War in Donbas

text: The insurgent-occupied town of Snizhne was hit by rockets fired from an aeroplane on 15 July, leaving at least 11 people dead, and destroying multiple homes.
title: War in Donbas

text: The insurgents blamed the Air Force of Ukraine, but the Ukrainian government denied any involvement.
title: War in Donbas

text: Clashes broke out between Ukrainian and pro-Russian forces along the border with Russia in Shakhtarsk Raion on 16 July.
title: War in Donbas

text: Insurgents who had been holed up in the town of Stepanivka made an attempt to escape encirclement by government forces at 05:00.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to a report by the National Guard, a roadblock near the border village of Marynivka was attacked by the insurgents with tanks, mortar fire, and anti-tank missiles.
title: War in Donbas

text: The checkpoint was shelled for over an hour, causing significant damage to infrastructure in Marynivka.
title: War in Donbas

text: Guardsmen repelled the attack, and forced the insurgents back to Stepanivka, where fighting continued.
title: War in Donbas

text: The battle then moved to the nearby village of Tarany.
title: War in Donbas

text: At least 11 Ukrainian soldiers died in the fighting.
title: War in Donbas

text: Attempts to form a "contact group" between the insurgents and the Ukrainian government, part of President Poroshenko's "15-point peace plan", failed, leaving little hope of a renewed ceasefire.
title: War in Donbas

text: This disaster followed two similar incidents earlier in the week, when two Ukrainian Air Force planes were shot down.
title: War in Donbas

text: DPR-affiliated insurgents blamed the Ukrainian government for the disaster, whereas the government, Netherlands, and Australia blamed Russia and the insurgents.
title: War in Donbas

text: The responsibility for investigation was delegated to the Dutch Safety Board (DSB) and the Dutch-led joint investigation team (JIT), who concluded that the airliner was downed by a Buk surface-to-air missile launched from pro-Russian separatist-controlled territory in Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: It said that a barrage of rockets hit "virtually every district".
title: War in Donbas

text: The shelling forced OSCE monitors to flee from their office in Luhansk, and move to Starobilsk.
title: War in Donbas

text: Shelling damaged an electrical substation in the city's Kamennobrodskiy district, causing blackouts.
title: War in Donbas

text: An oil refinery in Lysychansk was also set alight.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian forces went on to capture the south-eastern section of Luhansk city.
title: War in Donbas

text: Another 16 people were killed overnight, and at least 60 were wounded.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to a government report, Luhansk airport was secured by Ukrainian forces amidst the battle.
title: War in Donbas

text: Heavy fighting also resumed around Donetsk airport on 18 July, and explosions were heard in all districts of the city.
title: War in Donbas

text: By 21 July, heavy fighting in Donetsk resumed; the city was rocked by explosions and artillery fire.
title: War in Donbas

text: Fighting was concentrated in the northwestern districts of Kyivskyi and Kuibyshevskyi, and also near the central railway station and airport, leading local residents to seek refuge in bomb shelters, or to flee the city.
title: War in Donbas

text: The city's water supply was cut off during the fighting, and all railway and bus service was stopped.
title: War in Donbas

text: The streets emptied, and insurgents erected barricades across the city to control traffic.
title: War in Donbas

text: The cities of Dzerzhynsk, Soledar, and Rubizhne were retaken by Ukrainian forces on 21 July.
title: War in Donbas

text: The suburb of Mayorsk, just outside Horlivka, and the city of Sievierodonetsk, in Luhansk Oblast, were recaptured by Ukraine on 22 July.
title: War in Donbas

text: OSCE monitors visiting Donetsk following the previous day's fighting there said that the city was "practically deserted", and that the fighting had stopped.
title: War in Donbas

text: The same day, DPR prime minister Alexander Borodai said that he wanted to resume ceasefire talks.
title: War in Donbas

text: DPR commander Igor Girkin also said "The time has come when Russia must take a final decision – to really support Donbas's Russians or abandon them forever".
title: War in Donbas

text: Also, After having retaken Sievierodonetsk, government forces fought insurgents around the neighbouring city of Lysychansk.
title: War in Donbas

text: An insurgent car bomb killed three soldiers during the fighting there.
title: War in Donbas

text: Grad rocket attacks were launched against government forces garrisoned at Vesela Hora, Kamysheve, and also Luhansk airport.
title: War in Donbas

text: The press centre for the government military operation said that situation remained "most complex" in the areas around "Donetsk city, Luhansk city, Krasnodon and Popasna".
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian forces broke through the insurgent blockade around Donetsk airport on 23 July and then advanced into the northwestern corner of Donetsk city.
title: War in Donbas

text: The insurgents withdrew from many areas on the outskirts of the city.
title: War in Donbas

text: Insurgent commander Igor Girkin said that this was done to fortify Donetsk city centre, and also to avoid being encircled by government forces.
title: War in Donbas

text: He also said that he did not expect a government incursion into Donetsk city centre.
title: War in Donbas

text: Meanwhile, clashes continued in Shakhtarsk Raion, along the border with Russia.
title: War in Donbas

text: Amidst the fighting, two Ukrainian Su-25 fighter jets that had been providing air support to ground forces near Dmytrivka were shot down by the insurgents.
title: War in Donbas

text: Government forces launched air and artillery strikes on insurgents within the city, and clashes were fought all around it.
title: War in Donbas

text: One important bridge collapsed in the fighting, severing a critical route out of the city.
title: War in Donbas

text: People fled the violence in cars and on foot.
title: War in Donbas

text: Despite these advances by Ukrainian forces, the border with Russia was not secured.
title: War in Donbas

text: Izvaryne border post in Luhansk Oblast, which is controlled by the Army of the South-East, was reported to be the main entry point for weapons and reinforcements from Russia.
title: War in Donbas

text: Shelling began again in the Kyivskyi, Kirovskyi and Petrovskyi districts of Donetsk city.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to Donetsk city administration, 11 houses were damaged in Petrovsky, and at least one man was injured.
title: War in Donbas

text: The fighting continued overnight into 26 July, with explosions, shelling, and shooting heard across the city.
title: War in Donbas

text: During the third day of the government's offensive on the insurgent-stronghold of Horlivka, between 20 and 30 civilians were killed on 27 July.
title: War in Donbas

text: Horlivka was virtually abandoned, with electric power and water cut off.
title: War in Donbas

text: Shelling damaged or destroyed many buildings, including a hospital, greengrocer's, and energy company office.
title: War in Donbas

text: By 29 July, a further 17 civilians had been killed in the fighting in Horlivka, along with an additional 43 people injured.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 27 July, Ukrainian troops entered the town of Shakhtarsk, fought the insurgents that had been occupying it, and captured it.
title: War in Donbas

text: This cut off the supply corridor between the territories held by the DPR and LPR, isolating insurgents in Donetsk city.
title: War in Donbas

text: Skirmishes also broke out in the nearby towns of Snizhne and Torez.
title: War in Donbas

text: The insurgents allowed them to flee Ukraine, and cross into Russia.
title: War in Donbas

text: By 28 July, the strategic heights of Savur-Mohyla were under Ukrainian control, along with the town of Debaltseve.
title: War in Donbas

text: Insurgents had used Savur-Mohyla to shell Ukrainian troops around the town of Marynivka.
title: War in Donbas

text: Shelling continued in the Leninskyi and Kyivskyi districts of Donetsk city on 29 July.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to the city administration, these districts were heavily damaged.
title: War in Donbas

text: Government forces made a further advance on 30 July, when they evicted insurgents from Avdiivka, near Donetsk airport.
title: War in Donbas

text: This was meant to allow international experts to examine the crash site of Malaysia Airlines Flight 17, which is located in Shakhtarsk Raion, where the fiercest battles had been taking place on the previous few days.
title: War in Donbas

text: Monitors were escorted to the site by the Armed Forces of Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: After fighting severed various transmission lines, Luhansk city lost all access to electrical power on 31 July.
title: War in Donbas

text: Meanwhile, talks between the separatists, Russia, Ukraine, and the OSCE were held in Minsk.
title: War in Donbas

text: An ambush by the insurgents on government forces there resulted in the deaths of ten soldiers. 11 went missing, and 13 were wounded.
title: War in Donbas

text: A government offensive on the city of Pervomaisk in Luhansk Oblast continued.
title: War in Donbas

text: He addressed Russian president Vladimir Putin, saying that "Losing this war on the territory that President Vladimir Putin personally named New Russia would threaten the Kremlin's power and, personally, the power of the president".
title: War in Donbas

text: Government forces closed in on Luhansk and Donetsk cities on 3 August.
title: War in Donbas

text: A number of civilians were killed in fighting in both cities.
title: War in Donbas

text: Luhansk was reported to be "virtually surrounded", with little electrical power or water supply available.
title: War in Donbas

text: The situation in the city of Donetsk was less dire, as trains to Russia were still running, but fighting and shelling did not relent.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to the Ukrainian military, three-quarters of the territory once held by the insurgents had been recaptured.
title: War in Donbas

text: They also said that they had completely cut off supply lines between the DPR and LPR, after more than a week of fighting in Shakhtarsk Raion.
title: War in Donbas

text: After a prolonged battle, Ukrainian troops recaptured the vital town of Yasynuvata on 4 August.
title: War in Donbas

text: At least five soldiers died in the fighting to capture the town, which is a strategic railway junction on the main road between Donetsk and Luhansk cities.
title: War in Donbas

text: The pro-government paramilitary Azov and Shakhtarsk battalions said that they had advanced into Donetsk city, and had begun to "liberate" it.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Ukrainian government said that all civilians should evacuate from Donetsk, and issued statements asking DPR and LPR forces to help establish "humanitarian corridors" to allow civilians in Donetsk, Luhansk and Horlivka to flee.
title: War in Donbas

text: Commenting on the situation in Luhansk, mayor Sergei Kravchenko said "As a result of the blockade and ceaseless rocket attacks, the city is on the verge of a humanitarian catastrophe".
title: War in Donbas

text: As government troops pushed into Donetsk on 5 August, heavy fighting erupted at 17:00 in the Petrovskyi district of the city.
title: War in Donbas

text: Elsewhere, insurgents recaptured the town of Yasynuvata after a retreat by government forces.
title: War in Donbas

text: A spokesman from the National Security and Defence Council of Ukraine said that the Armed Forces left the town to avoid harming the "peaceful population", and that the city was being evacuated so that it could be "completely liberated".
title: War in Donbas

text: He also said that the railway station remained under government control, and that all railway traffic had been blocked.
title: War in Donbas

text: Fighting between insurgents and government forces across the Donbas region continued "constantly" over the course of the day.
title: War in Donbas

text: Fighting and shelling continued around Donetsk on 8 August, with several civilians killed or injured.
title: War in Donbas

text: By 9 August, insurgent commander Igor Girkin said that Donetsk had been "completely encircled" by government forces.
title: War in Donbas

text: This followed the capture of the vital town of Krasnyi Luch by the government, after insurgent-aligned Cossacks stationed there fled.
title: War in Donbas

text: Further skirmishes between insurgents and the Armed Forces took place in Mnohopillia, Stepanivka, Hryhorivka, Krasny Yar, Pobeda, Shyshkove, Komyshne, Novohannivka, Krasna Talivka, Dmytrivka, Sabivka, and Luhansk airport.
title: War in Donbas

text: Overnight and into 10 August, government forces launched an artillery barrage on Donetsk city.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to a spokesman for the Armed Forces, insurgents began to flee the city during the barrage, and were in a state of "panic and chaos".
title: War in Donbas

text: Hospitals and residential buildings were heavily damaged, and many remaining residents took shelter in basements.
title: War in Donbas

text: The cities of Pervomaisk, Kalynove, Komyshuvakha, in western Luhansk Oblast near Popasna, were captured by government forces on 12 August after heavy fighting.
title: War in Donbas

text: Heavy shelling of Donetsk continued into 14 August.
title: War in Donbas

text: During this artillery barrage, Igor Girkin resigned from his post as commander of the insurgent forces of the Donetsk People's Republic.
title: War in Donbas

text: He was replaced by Vladimir Kononov, who is known by the nom de guerre Tsar.
title: War in Donbas

text: Girkin's resignation, along with the 7 August resignation of DPR prime minister Alexander Borodai (who was replaced by Alexander Zakharchenko), represented a shift in the nature of the conflict.
title: War in Donbas

text: Given the recent military failings of the DPR and the LPR, Russia decided that it could no longer rely on a patchwork of irregular fighters in the Donbas, and ordered a change in leadership.
title: War in Donbas

text: It abandoned the separatist project, and replaced it with the idea of federalisation of Donbas within Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: NATO Secretary General Anders Fogh Rasmussen confirmed that a "Russian incursion" into Ukraine had occurred.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian president Petro Poroshenko said that Ukrainian artillery engaged and destroyed a "significant" portion of the armoured column.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Russian Defence Ministry denied the existence of any such convoy.
title: War in Donbas

text: Following this incident, the newly appointed prime minister of the DPR Alexander Zakharchenko said that his forces included 1,200 Russian-trained combatants.
title: War in Donbas

text: A Ukrainian Air Force MiG-29 fighter jet was shot down by the insurgents in Luhansk Oblast on 17 August.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ten civilians were killed during continued shelling in Donetsk.
title: War in Donbas

text: The insurgent-occupied city of Horlivka was encircled by the Armed Forces on 18 August.
title: War in Donbas

text: Government forces also advanced into the edges of Luhansk city.
title: War in Donbas

text: A convoy of refugees from Luhansk was hit by Grad rockets near the village of Novosvitlivka.
title: War in Donbas

text: Dozens of civilians died in the attack, which the National Security and Defence Council of Ukraine blamed on the insurgents.
title: War in Donbas

text: DPR prime minister Aleksandr Zakharchenko stated that if the Ukrainian government made "reasonable proposals to lay down arms, close borders, we will talk on equal terms as equal partners".
title: War in Donbas

text: He added, however, that the government "must recognise us as a state, now it is already impossible to ask for a certain degree of autonomy".
title: War in Donbas

text: After having edged into Luhansk city on 18 August, government forces began to advance through the city "block by block" on 19 August.
title: War in Donbas

text: Fighting was heard in streets across the city, and shelling of many insurgent-occupied districts continued.
title: War in Donbas

text: There was also fighting Makiivka and Ilovaisk, two cities just outside Donetsk city.
title: War in Donbas

text: A spokesman for the Internal Affairs Ministry said that government forces were "clearing" Ilovaisk of insurgents, and later captured most of the city.
title: War in Donbas

text: The headquarters of the DPR in Donetsk city were also shelled.
title: War in Donbas

text: Fighting across Donetsk Oblast on 19 August resulted in the deaths of 34 civilians.
title: War in Donbas

text: By early evening on 20 August, government forces said that they had recaptured "significant parts" of the city of Luhansk, after a series of running battles in streets throughout the day.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 23 August heavily armed men, believed to be Russian troops, erected a roadblock southwest of Amvrosiivka, in the vicinity of Kolosky, approximately 10 kilometers from the border.
title: War in Donbas

text: The next day, Ukrainian media reported that Russian army armoured forces equipped with 250 vehicles and artillery had entered Amvrosiivka, in what seemed to be the beginning of a major offensive on Mariupol.
title: War in Donbas

text: By 25 August, an insurgent counter-offensive had stalled the government's advance on Donetsk and Luhansk cities.
title: War in Donbas

text: Insurgents attacked government positions in Shchastia, and along the Siverskyi Donets River in Luhansk Oblast.
title: War in Donbas

text: As this attack occurred, insurgents in Luhansk received reinforcements.
title: War in Donbas

text: Government forces near Ilovaisk and Amvrosiivka in Donetsk Oblast became surrounded by insurgents, after their attempt to take Ilovaisk was halted by heavy shelling.
title: War in Donbas

text: The pro-government volunteer Donbas Battalion, trapped in the city for days by the insurgents, accused the Ukrainian government and Armed Forces of "abandoning" them.
title: War in Donbas

text: Other volunteer battalions, such as the Azov and Dnipro, left Ilovaisk after encountering heavy resistance.
title: War in Donbas

text: Donbas Battalion leader Semen Semenchenko said "I think it is profitable for the defence ministry not to send help, but to achieve a situation where volunteer battalions start blaming each other about who helped who".
title: War in Donbas

text: A column of armoured vehicles crossed into Ukraine from Russia near Novoazovsk on 25 August.
title: War in Donbas

text: There had been no insurgent formations within 30 kilometres (18+2⁄3 mi) of this area for many weeks.
title: War in Donbas

text: Heavy fighting took place in the village of Markyne, 7 kilometres (4+1⁄4 mi) from Novoazovsk.
title: War in Donbas

text: Insurgents used the village as a base to shell Novoazovsk.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to the Mariupol city website, the Dnipro and Donbas battalions repelled the attack, and the "invaders" retreated to the border.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov said he had no knowledge of the incident, and suggested that reports of the incident being an incursion by Russian forces were "disinformation".
title: War in Donbas

text: Directly prior to the appearance of the column, the area was heavily shelled.
title: War in Donbas

text: The nearest insurgent artillery positions were beyond the range of this area.
title: War in Donbas

text: Villagers from Kolosky in Starobesheve Raion told Reuters that military men with Russian accents and no identifying insignias had appeared in the village at the weekend of 23–24 August.
title: War in Donbas

text: They set up a roadblock near the village.
title: War in Donbas

text: The villagers referred to them as "little green men", a term that was used to refer to the irregular Russian forces that took control of Crimea from February 2014.
title: War in Donbas

text: Following the appearance of these men, ten soldiers in green military uniforms with white armbands were detained by Ukrainian forces at Dzerkalne.
title: War in Donbas

text: This village is north of Novoazovosk, 7 kilometres (4+1⁄4 mi) from Kolosky, and about 20 kilometres (12 mi) from the Russian border.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Russian military confirmed that these men were Russian paratroopers and that they had been captured.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Russian Defence Ministry said the men had entered Ukraine "by mistake during an exercise".
title: War in Donbas

text: The Security Service of Ukraine (SBU) released videos that they said were interviews with the captive Russian soldiers.
title: War in Donbas

text: Whilst the Ukrainian government said they were in "total control" of Novoazovsk, town mayor Oleg Sidorkin confirmed that the insurgents had captured it.
title: War in Donbas

text: He also said that "dozens" of tanks and armoured vehicles had been used by the insurgents in their assault on the town.
title: War in Donbas

text: At least four civilians were injured by insurgent shelling.
title: War in Donbas

text: To the north, close to Starobesheve, Ukrainian forces said that they spotted a column of 100 armoured vehicles, tanks, and Grad rocket lorries that was heading south, toward Novoazovsk.
title: War in Donbas

text: They said these vehicles were marked with "white circles or triangles", similar to the white armbands seen on the captured Russian paratroopers earlier in the week.
title: War in Donbas

text: Amidst pressure on this new third front, government forces retreated westward toward Mariupol.
title: War in Donbas

text: They evacuated the town of Starobesheve, among other areas in the 75-kilometre (47 mi) stretch of borderland from the Sea of Azov to the existing insurgent-held territories.
title: War in Donbas

text: A report by The New York Times described the retreating soldiers as "exhausted, filthy and dismayed".
title: War in Donbas

text: Western officials described the new insurgent actions as a "stealth invasion" by the Russian Federation, with tanks, artillery and infantry said to have crossed into Ukraine from Russian territory.
title: War in Donbas

text: US State Department spokesman Jen Psaki said that "these incursions indicate a Russian-directed counteroffensive is likely underway", and Ukrainian president Petro Poroshenko said "An invasion of Russian forces has taken place".
title: War in Donbas

text: A statement by the National Security and Defence Council of Ukraine (NSDC) later said that Novoazovsk had been captured by "Russian troops", despite earlier denials by the Ukrainian government.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to the NSDC, Ukrainian troops withdrew from Novoazovsk to save lives, and were instead preparing defences in Mariupol.
title: War in Donbas

text: Meanwhile, fighting continued in and around Donetsk city.
title: War in Donbas

text: Shells fell on the Kalininskyi district of Donetsk, and the Donbas Battalion continued to fight against the insurgents that had trapped them in Ilovaisk for days.
title: War in Donbas

text: Nico Tak said on 28 August that "well over" 1,000 Russian soldiers were operating in the Donbas conflict zone.
title: War in Donbas

text: Amidst what The New York Times described as "chaos" in the conflict zone, the insurgents re-captured Savur-Mohyla.
title: War in Donbas

text: Despite these advances by pro-Russian forces, the National Guard of Ukraine temporarily retook the city of Komsomolske in Starobesheve Raion of Donetsk Oblast on 29 August.
title: War in Donbas

text: However, two days later, Ukrainian forces retreated from the city, and Komsomolske was once again taken by the DPR forces.
title: War in Donbas

text: Elsewhere, Ukrainian forces retreated from Novosvitlivka after being attacked by what they said were "Russian tanks".
title: War in Donbas

text: They said that every house in the village was destroyed.
title: War in Donbas

text: The trapped Donbas Battalion withdrew from Ilovaisk on 30 August after negotiating an agreement with pro-Russian forces.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to some of the troops who withdrew from Ilovaisk, DPR forces violated the agreement and fired on them whilst they retreated under white flags, killing as many as several dozen.
title: War in Donbas

text: A Ukrainian patrol boat in the Sea of Azov was hit by shore-based artillery fire on 31 August.
title: War in Donbas

text: Eight sailors were rescued from the sinking boat, whilst two crew-members were missing.
title: War in Donbas

text: Former insurgent commander Igor Girkin said that the insurgents had "dealt the enemy their first naval defeat".
title: War in Donbas

text: Government forces withdrew from Luhansk International Airport on 1 September, despite having held the airport from insurgent attacks for weeks prior.
title: War in Donbas
