text: The airport saw fierce fighting on the night before the withdrawal, and Ukrainian officials said that their forces at the airport had been attacked by a column of Russian tanks.
title: War in Donbas

text: Heavy fighting was observed by OSCE monitors near the villages of Shyrokyne and Bezimenne on 4 September.
title: War in Donbas

text: Respectively, these villages are 24 kilometres (15 mi) and 34 kilometres (21 mi) east of Mariupol.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian officials in Mariupol said that the situation there "was worsening by the hour", and that there was an imminent danger of an attack on the city.
title: War in Donbas

text: DPR forces came within 5 kilometres (3 mi) of the city on 4 September, but their advance was repulsed by an overnight counter-attack launched by the Ukrainian military and the Azov Battalion.
title: War in Donbas

text: They were driven back about 20 kilometres (12+1⁄2 mi) east of the city.
title: War in Donbas

text: Humanitarian corridors were meant to be maintained so that civilians could leave affected areas.
title: War in Donbas

text: Use of the Russian language in the Donbas would be protected by law.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to The New York Times, the agreement was an "almost verbatim" replication of Ukrainian president Petro Poroshenko's failed June "15-point peace plan".
title: War in Donbas

text: Russia started a more robust train and equip operation to strengthen separatists forces.
title: War in Donbas

text: DPR and LPR leaders said that they retained their desire for full independence from Ukraine, despite these concessions.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russian president Vladimir Putin and Ukrainian president Poroshenko discussed the ceasefire on 6 September.
title: War in Donbas

text: Both parties said that they were satisfied with the ceasefire, and that it was generally holding.
title: War in Donbas

text: The ceasefire was broken multiple times on the night of 6–7 September, and into the day on 7 September.
title: War in Donbas

text: These violations resulted in the deaths of four Ukrainian soldiers, whilst 29 were injured.
title: War in Donbas

text: Heavy shelling by the insurgents was reported on the eastern outskirts of Mariupol, and OSCE monitors said that the Ukrainian government had fired rockets from Donetsk International Airport.
title: War in Donbas

text: The OSCE said that these breaches of the agreement would not cause the ceasefire to collapse.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian president Petro Poroshenko said on 10 September that "70% of Russian troops have been moved back across the border", and also added that this action gave him "hope that the peace initiatives have good prospects".
title: War in Donbas

text: In line with the Minsk Protocol, OSCE monitors said that they observed a prisoner exchange near Avdiivka on 12 September.
title: War in Donbas

text: OSCE monitors documented violations of the Minsk Protocol in numerous areas of Donetsk Oblast from 13 to 15 September.
title: War in Donbas

text: Two of the armoured vehicles that the monitors were travelling in were struck by shrapnel, rendering one of the vehicles inoperable and forcing the monitors to retreat.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to the monitors, troop and equipment movements were being carried out by both DPR and Ukrainian forces.
title: War in Donbas

text: They also said that there were "command and control issues" amongst both parties to the conflict.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian president Petro Poroshenko said on 21 September that the Armed Forces of Ukraine lost between 60% and 65% of its total active equipment over the course of the war.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to the OSCE on 25 September, all parties agreed that the fighting had "subsided in recent days", and that the "situation along 70%" of the buffer zone was "calm".
title: War in Donbas

text: In the most significant incident since the start of the ceasefire, seven Ukrainian soldiers died on 29 September when a tank shell struck the armoured personnel carrier that they were travelling in near Donetsk International Airport.
title: War in Donbas

text: Over the next few days, fighting continued around Donetsk International Airport, whilst Donetsk city itself came under heavy shelling.
title: War in Donbas

text: Amidst this renewed violence, OSCE chairman Didier Burkhalter issued a statement that "urged all sides to immediately stop fighting".
title: War in Donbas

text: According to a report released by the UN Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR) on 8 October, the ceasefire implemented by the Minsk Protocol was becoming "increasingly fragile".
title: War in Donbas

text: It said that at least 331 people had been killed since the start of ceasefire, and that the most fierce fighting took place around Donetsk International Airport, Debaltseve, and Shchastia.
title: War in Donbas

text: However, the separatists then announced their own elections in November.
title: War in Donbas

text: The OSCE chairman Didier Burkhalter said this violated the Minsk agreement and would "further complicate its implementation".
title: War in Donbas

text: The elections were not agreed to by all sides, would not involve Ukrainian parties and candidates, would not be observed by the OSCE, and would be held while Russian-backed militants controlled the region.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russia said it "respected" the vote, treating the new authorities as representing the "people of Donbas" in future negotiations.
title: War in Donbas

text: In response to the unlawful elections, Ukrainian president Poroshenko asked parliament to revoke the "special status" granted to rebel-held areas.
title: War in Donbas

text: DPR deputy prime minister Andrei Purgin then said that Ukrainian forces had launched "all-out war" against the DPR and LPR on 6 November.
title: War in Donbas

text: Concurrently, separatist representatives requested a re-drafting of the Minsk Protocol.
title: War in Donbas

text: Intermittent shelling of Donetsk renewed on 5 November.
title: War in Donbas

text: OSCE monitors reported on 8 November that there were large movements of unmarked heavy equipment in separatist-held territory.
title: War in Donbas

text: These included armoured personnel carriers, lorries, petrol tankers, and tanks, which were being manned and escorted by men in dark green uniforms without insignias.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian government spokesmen said that these were Russian troops, but this could not be independently verified.
title: War in Donbas

text: Overnight into 9 November, intense shelling from both government and insurgent positions rocked Donetsk.
title: War in Donbas

text: OSCE chairman Didier Burkhalter said that he was "very concerned" about the "resurgence of violence".
title: War in Donbas

text: OSCE monitors observed more munitions convoys in separatist-held territory on 9 November.
title: War in Donbas

text: These included 17 unmarked green ZiL lorries loaded with ammunition at Sverdlovsk, and 17 similar Kamaz lorries towing howitzers at Zuhres.
title: War in Donbas

text: Another convoy of 43 green military lories, some towing howitzers and rocket launchers, was observed by OSCE monitors in Donetsk on 11 November.
title: War in Donbas

text: Following the reports of these troop and equipment movements, NATO General Philip Breedlove said on 12 November that Russian troops and heavy equipment had crossed into Ukraine during the preceding week.
title: War in Donbas

text: In response, the Ukrainian Defence Ministry said that it was preparing for a renewed offensive by pro-Russian forces.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russian Defence Ministry spokesman Major General Igor Konashenkov said "there was and is no evidence" to support NATO's statement.
title: War in Donbas

text: By 2 December, at least 1,000 people had died during fighting in the Donbas, since the signing of the Minsk Protocol in early September.
title: War in Donbas

text: A BBC report said that the ceasefire had been "a fiction".
title: War in Donbas

text: A report by the International Crisis Group stated that the late 2014 financial crisis in Russia, in tandem with American and European economic sanctions, deterred further advances by pro-Russian forces.
title: War in Donbas

text: The report also raised concerns about the potential for "humanitarian catastrophe" in separatist-controlled Donbas during the cold winter months, saying that the separatists were unable "to provide basic services for the population".
title: War in Donbas

text: In line with the Minsk Protocol, more prisoner exchanges took place during the week of 21–27 December.
title: War in Donbas

text: More OSCE-organised talks were held in Minsk during that week, but they reached no result.
title: War in Donbas

text: In a press conference on 29 December, Ukrainian President Petro Poroshenko said that the Minsk Protocol was becoming effective "point by point", and also said that "progress" was being made.
title: War in Donbas

text: Since the signing of the Protocol, over 1,500 people held by the separatists had been released as part of the prisoner exchanges.
title: War in Donbas

text: Whereas Ukrainian forces had been losing about 100 men per day prior to the Protocol, only about 200 had been killed in the four months since its signing.
title: War in Donbas

text: Numerous ceasefire violations were recorded, with most occurring near Donetsk International Airport.
title: War in Donbas

text: Infighting amongst insurgent groups broke out in Luhansk Oblast.
title: War in Donbas

text: In one incident, LPR militants said that they had killed Alexander Bednov, the leader of the pro-Russian "Batman Battalion", on 2 January 2015.
title: War in Donbas

text: LPR officials said that Bednov had been running an "illegal prison", and that he had engaged in torturing prisoners.
title: War in Donbas

text: An intercity bus stopped at a government checkpoint in Buhas was hit by a Grad rocket on 13 January, killing 12 civilians (see Volnovakha bus attack).
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian president Petro Poroshenko declared a day of national mourning.
title: War in Donbas

text: The new terminal building at Donetsk International Airport, which had been a site of fighting between Ukrainian and separatist troops since May 2014, was captured by the DPR forces on 15 January.
title: War in Donbas

text: In the days prior to the capturing, the airport was heavily barraged by separatist rocket fire.
title: War in Donbas

text: DPR leader Alexander Zakharchenko stated that the capture of the airport was the first step toward regaining territory lost to Ukrainian forces during the middle of 2014.
title: War in Donbas

text: He said "Let our countrymen hear this: We will not just give up our land.
title: War in Donbas

text: We will either take it back peacefully, or like that", referring to the capture of the airport.
title: War in Donbas

text: Such an offensive by separatist forces would signal the complete breakdown of the ceasefire.
title: War in Donbas

text: Concurrently, a new round of Minsk talks, scheduled for 16 January by the Trilateral Contact Group on Ukraine, was called off after DPR and LPR leaders Alexander Zakharchenko and Igor Plotnitsky refused to attend.
title: War in Donbas

text: A government military operation at the weekend of 17–18 January resulted in Ukrainian forces recapturing most of Donetsk International Airport.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to Ukrainian NSDC representative Andriy Lysenko, the operation restored the lines of control established by the Minsk Protocol, and therefore did not constitute a violation of it.
title: War in Donbas

text: The operation caused fighting to move toward Donetsk proper, resulting in heavy shelling of residential areas of the city that border the airport.
title: War in Donbas

text: DPR authorities said that they halted government forces at Putylivskiy bridge, which connects the airport and the city proper.
title: War in Donbas

text: The bridge, which is strategically important, was destroyed during the fighting.
title: War in Donbas

text: OSCE monitors reported that shelling had caused heavy damage in the Donetsk residential districts of Kyivskyi, Kirovskyi, Petrovskyi, and Voroshilovskyi.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian President Petro Poroshenko said on 21 January that Russia had deployed more than 9,000 soldiers and 500 tanks, artillery units, and armoured personnel carriers in the Donbas.
title: War in Donbas

text: An article that appeared in The Daily Telegraph said that deployment appeared to be "a response to Kyiv's success" in retaining control of Donetsk International Airport.
title: War in Donbas

text: The same day, Ukrainian forces attempted to surround the airport in an attempt to push back the insurgents.
title: War in Donbas

text: As Ukrainian and DPR forces fought away from the airport, a group of insurgents stormed the first and third floors of the new terminal building.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian troops held out on the second floor of the building until the ceiling collapsed, killing several soldiers.
title: War in Donbas

text: The remaining Ukrainian forces were either captured, killed, or were forced to withdraw from the airport, allowing DPR forces to overrun it.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to one volunteer, 37 Ukrainian troops died.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Daily Telegraph called the Ukrainian defeat at the airport "devastating".
title: War in Donbas

text: Following this victory, separatist forces began to attack Ukrainian forces along the line of control in Donetsk and Luhansk oblasts.
title: War in Donbas

text: Particularly heavy fighting broke out along the Siverskyi Donets River, to the north-west of Luhansk city.
title: War in Donbas

text: Separatist forces captured a Ukrainian checkpoint at Krymske, attacked other checkpoints in the area, and shelled villages near Shchastia.
title: War in Donbas

text: Separatist forces began an assault on the government-controlled town of Debaltseve, barraging it with artillery fire.
title: War in Donbas

text: The DPR launched an attack on Mariupol from Shyrokyne on 24 January.
title: War in Donbas

text: A hail of Grad rockets killed at least 30 people, and wounded another 83.
title: War in Donbas

text: Heavy fighting continued in Debaltseve over the next week, resulting in many civilian and combatant casualties.
title: War in Donbas

text: French president François Hollande and German chancellor Angela Merkel put forth a new peace plan on 7 February.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Franco-German plan, drawn up after talks with Ukrainian president Petro Poroshenko and Russian president Vladimir Putin, was seen as a revival of the Minsk Protocol.
title: War in Donbas

text: President Hollande said that the plan was the "last chance" for resolution of the conflict.
title: War in Donbas

text: The plan was put forth in response to American proposals to send armaments to the Ukrainian government, something that Chancellor Merkel said would only result in a worsening of the crisis.
title: War in Donbas

text: Fighting worsened in the run-up to the scheduled 11 February talks to discuss the Franco-German peace plan.
title: War in Donbas

text: DPR forces shelled the city of Kramatorsk on 10 February, which had last seen fighting in July 2014.
title: War in Donbas

text: The shelling targeted the city's Armed Forces headquarters, but also hit a nearby residential area.
title: War in Donbas

text: Seven people were killed, while 26 were wounded.
title: War in Donbas

text: The pro-government Azov Battalion launched an offensive to recapture separatist-controlled areas on the outskirts of Mariupol, centred on the village of Shyrokyne.
title: War in Donbas

text: The plan, similar in content to the failed Minsk Protocol, called for an unconditional ceasefire, to begin on 15 February, amongst many other measures.
title: War in Donbas

text: Despite the signing of Minsk II, fighting continued around Debaltseve.
title: War in Donbas

text: DPR forces said that ceasefire did not apply to Debaltseve, and continued their offensive.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian forces were forced to withdraw from the Debaltseve area on 18 February, leaving separatist forces in control of it.
title: War in Donbas

text: In the week after the fall of Debaltseve to pro-Russian forces, fighting in the conflict zone abated.
title: War in Donbas

text: DPR and LPR forces began to withdraw artillery from the front lines as specified by Minsk II on 24 February, and Ukraine did so on 26 February.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukraine reported that it had suffered no casualties during 24–26 February, something that had not occurred since early January 2015.
title: War in Donbas

text: Minor skirmishes continued into March, but the ceasefire was largely observed across the combat zone.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian and separatist forces had withdrawn most of the heavy weaponry specified in Minsk II by 10 March.
title: War in Donbas

text: Minor violations of the ceasefire continued throughout March and into April, though it continued to hold, and the numbers of casualties reported by both sides were greatly reduced.
title: War in Donbas

text: Fighting flared up on 3 June 2015, when DPR insurgents launched an attack on government-controlled Marinka.
title: War in Donbas

text: Artillery and tanks were utilised in the battle there, which was described as the heaviest fighting since the signing of Minsk II.
title: War in Donbas

text: An anti-war protest took place in Donetsk city on 15 June.
title: War in Donbas

text: The protest, the first of its kind in pro-Russian separatist-controlled territory, called for an end to the fighting in the Donbas.
title: War in Donbas

text: Whilst all parties to the conflict continued to support implementation of the measures specified by Minsk II, minor skirmishes continued on a daily basis through June and July 2015.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian troops suffered losses on a daily basis, and the ceasefire was labelled "unworkable" and "impossible to implement".
title: War in Donbas

text: Despite constant fighting and shelling along the line of contact, no territorial changes occurred.
title: War in Donbas

text: This state of stalemate led the war to be labelled a "frozen conflict".
title: War in Donbas

text: Following months of ceasefire violations, the Ukrainian government, the DPR and the LPR jointly agreed to halt all fighting, starting on 1 September 2015.
title: War in Donbas

text: This agreement coincided with the start of the school year in Ukraine, and was intended to allow for another attempt at implementing the points of Minsk II.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to Ukrainian Defence Minister Stepan Poltorak, violence in the Donbas had reached its lowest level since the start of the war.
title: War in Donbas

text: Whilst the ceasefire continued to hold into November, no final settlement to the conflict was agreed.
title: War in Donbas

text: Within days both sides accused each other of breaching the ceasefire, although they also stated that the ceasefire was widely observed.
title: War in Donbas

text: Nevertheless, on 6 September (2016), Ukrainian authorities reported the death of yet another soldier.
title: War in Donbas

text: In 2017, on average a Ukrainian soldier died every three days, with an estimated 40,000 separatist and 6,000 Russian troops in the region.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 18 February 2017, Russian president Vladimir Putin signed a decree whereby the Russian authorities would recognise personal and vehicle-registration documents issued by the DPR and LPR.
title: War in Donbas

text: The presidential decree referred to "permanent residents of certain areas of Ukraine's Donetsk and Luhansk Oblasts", without any mention of the self-proclaimed People's Republics.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian authorities decried the decree as being directly contradictory to the Minsk II agreement and that it "legally recognised the quasi-state terrorist groups which cover Russia's occupation of part of Donbas".
title: War in Donbas

text: Russian foreign minister Sergey Lavrov, after meeting with his Ukrainian, German and French counterparts in Munich on 18 February, said that a ceasefire between Ukraine and the separatists had been agreed effective from 20 February 2017.
title: War in Donbas

text: But according to a Ukrainian Armed Forces spokesman on 20 February 2017 separatists attacks continued, although he did state there was a "significant reduction in military activity".
title: War in Donbas

text: On 21 February OSCE's Secretary General Zannier stated there were still a significant number of violations of the cease-fire and "no evidence of the withdrawal of weapons".
title: War in Donbas

text: According to both parties to the conflict, the fourth truce attempt of 2017 collapsed within a few hours on 24 June 2017.
title: War in Donbas

text: A "back to school ceasefire" to begin on 25 August 2017 also immediately collapsed.
title: War in Donbas

text: A further "Christmas ceasefire" on 23 December 2017 was immediately broken.).
title: War in Donbas

text: On 27 December 2017, as part of the Minsk deal, a prisoner swap was conducted with 73 Ukrainian soldiers exchanged for over 200 separatists.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 18 January 2018, the Ukrainian parliament passed a bill to regain control over separatist-held areas.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Russian government denounced the bill, calling it "preparations for a new war", and accused the Ukrainian government of violating the Minsk agreement.
title: War in Donbas

text: The law on the reintegration of Donbas labeled the republics of Donetsk and Luhansk as "temporarily-occupied territories", while Russia was labeled as an "aggressor".
title: War in Donbas

text: The legislation granted President Poroshenko "the right to use military force inside the country, without consent from the Ukrainian parliament", which would include the reclaiming of Donbas.
title: War in Donbas

text: The bill supports a ban on trade and a transport blockade of the east that has been in place since 2017.
title: War in Donbas

text: Under the legislation, the only separatist-issued documents that Ukraine would recognise are birth and death certificates.
title: War in Donbas

text: A new ceasefire began on 5 March 2018, but was soon broken.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Trilateral Contact Group on Ukraine agreed on a "comprehensive, sustainable and unlimited ceasefire" that was to start on 30 March 2018.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukraine officially ended the "Anti-Terrorist Operation" (ATO), and replaced it with "Joint Forces Operation" (JFO) on 30 April 2018.
title: War in Donbas

text: Lieutenant-General Serhii Naiev, commander of the Joint Forces Operation, said the renaming was intended to signify that Ukraine was not fighting against indigenous "terrorists" or "separatist militants" in the Donbas, but against the Russian military.
title: War in Donbas

text: The same day, the United States confirmed that it had delivered Javelin anti-tank missiles to Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to The Washington Post, the missiles will be kept away from the front line, and would be used only in the case of an all-out separatist assault.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 28 June 2018, a "harvest ceasefire" was agreed set to start on 1 July.
title: War in Donbas

text: Both sides accused each other of violating it within hours.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 31 August 2018, DPR leader Alexander Zakharchenko was killed in an explosion at a restaurant.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 28 December 2018, Ukraine's Chief of the General Staff, Viktor Muzhenko, said that almost the entire "grey zone" between the warring sides had been liberated from Russian-led forces without breaching the Minsk peace agreements.
title: War in Donbas

text: The same day, a new (and the 22nd attempt at an) ceasefire starting midnight 29 December was agreed.
title: War in Donbas

text: Both sides accused each other of violating the ceasefire within hours.
title: War in Donbas

text: A new ceasefire was agreed to start on 8 March 2019.
title: War in Donbas

text: Although Ukraine claimed that "Russian proxies" violated it on the same day, fighting died down, with the Ukrainian side stating that the ceasefire was fully observed from 10 March 2019.
title: War in Donbas

text: In June 2019, Russia began distributing Russian passports to Ukrainians living in the regions of Donbas.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukraine, Russia, the DPR, LPR, and the OSCE signed an agreement on 1 October 2019 to try to end the conflict.
title: War in Donbas

text: Called the "Steinmeier formula", after its proposer, German President Frank-Walter Steinmeier, it was a simplified version of the Minsk agreements.
title: War in Donbas

text: The plan called for holding elections, under Ukrainian law, in the rebel-held territory.
title: War in Donbas

text: If the OSCE deemed the elections free and fair, the territory would be re-integrated into Ukraine with "special status".
title: War in Donbas

text: Russia demanded the agreement's signing before any continuation of the "Normandy format" peace talks.
title: War in Donbas

text: In line with the agreement, Ukrainian and pro-Russian forces agreed to pull back from three parts of the frontline: at Stanytsia Luhanska, Zolote and Petrivske.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukraine's new president Volodomyr Zelenskyy faced criticism from Ukrainian media, civil society, war veterans and nationalists.
title: War in Donbas

text: Critics argued that the Steinmeier plan would legalize the occupation of the Donbas and leave it as "a deadly Trojan horse under Russian control".
title: War in Donbas

text: The main issues were holding elections in Donbas while it and the border were still controlled by Russian forces.
title: War in Donbas

text: In these conditions, it was argued that elections were unlikely to be free and fair, and it would be impossible to ensure Russia kept its end of the agreement.
title: War in Donbas

text: The separatists had long driven most pro-Ukrainian residents out of the region to ensure a pro-Russia majority.
title: War in Donbas

text: The leaders of the Donbas separatists, Denis Pushilin and Leonid Pasechnik, stated: "It will be we who decide ... how our people's militia will be defending our citizens, and how we will be integrated into Russia. ...
title: War in Donbas

text: Kyiv authorities will not get any sort of control over the border".
title: War in Donbas

text: Zelenskyy responded that he would not allow elections "at gunpoint".
title: War in Donbas

text: He said there must first be a ceasefire, return of prisoners, withdrawal of Russian forces, Ukrainian control of its border, participation of Ukrainian parties and candidates, and the right of all those who fled the conflict to return to vote.
title: War in Donbas

text: Putin, Zelenskyy, French president Emmanuel Macron and German chancellor Angela Merkel met in Paris on 9 December 2019 in a resumption of the Normandy format talks.
title: War in Donbas

text: The two sides agreed to exchange all remaining prisoners by the end of 2019, work toward elections in the Donbas, and schedule further talks.
title: War in Donbas

text: However, Putin insisted that control of the border should only be dealt with after the elections.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Russian side continued to deny that its military was operating in the Donbas, instead calling them "Russian volunteers".
title: War in Donbas

text: The COVID-19 pandemic worsened living conditions in the conflict zone.
title: War in Donbas

text: Quarantine measures imposed by Ukraine, the DPR and LPR prevented those in the occupied territories from crossing the line of contact, cutting off access to services.
title: War in Donbas

text: Fighting increased in March 2020, with nineteen civilians killed, more than in the previous five months combined.
title: War in Donbas

text: While some crossings opened to small numbers of people in June, the DPR introduced new restrictions, ostensibly to prevent the spread of coronavirus, which made it nigh impossible for most to cross the line of contact.
title: War in Donbas

text: The 29th attempt at a "full and comprehensive" ceasefire came into effect on 27 July 2020.
title: War in Donbas

text: During his 24 August 2020 Ukrainian Independence Day speech, President Zelenskyy announced the ceasefire had held, leading to 29 days without combat losses.
title: War in Donbas

text: Zelenskyy also admitted, however, that despite the prisoner exchange and de-mining operations, the peace process did not move as fast as he had hoped.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 6 September 2020, the Ukrainian Armed Forces reported its first combat death since the July ceasefire.
title: War in Donbas

text: President Zelenskyy stated on 7 November 2020 that since the July ceasefire was established, deaths of Ukrainian soldiers in combat had decreased tenfold, and the number of attacks on soldiers decreased by five-and-a-half-fold.
title: War in Donbas

text: OSCE observers reported more than 90,000 ceasefire violations throughout 2021; the vast majority in Russian-controlled territory.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to Ukrainian authorities, 25 Ukrainian soldiers were killed in the first three months of 2021, compared to 50 killed in all of 2020.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to the Ombudsman of the DPR, 85 of its fighters and 30 civilians were killed in January–October 2021.
title: War in Donbas

text: In late March–early April 2021, the Russian military moved large quantities of arms and equipment into occupied Crimea and the Voronezh and Rostov oblasts of Russia.
title: War in Donbas

text: A Janes intelligence specialist called it the largest unannounced military movement since the 2014 invasion of Crimea.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 10 April, Russian state news agency TASS reported that fifty of its battalion tactical groups consisting of 15,000 soldiers were massed for drills in the Southern Military District, which includes occupied Crimea and also borders the Donbas conflict zone.
title: War in Donbas

text: The head of the Ukrainian border guard estimated that 85,000 Russian soldiers were already in Crimea or within 40 kilometres (25 mi) of the Ukrainian border.
title: War in Donbas

text: By this time, some half a million people in the self-proclaimed Donetsk People's Republic and Luhansk People's Republic had been issued Russian passports since fighting broke out in 2014.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russia refused to participate when Ukraine requested a Vienna Document meeting with France, Germany, and the OSCE.
title: War in Donbas

text: In April 2021, Ukraine performed the first operational rollout of Turkish-made Bayraktar TB2 military drones in the region.
title: War in Donbas

text: In November, a Bayraktar drone on the Ukrainian-government-controlled side of the line of contact was used to destroy a separatist artillery piece on the other side, which was conducting a strike that levelled homes and wounded and killed Ukrainian soldiers.
title: War in Donbas

text: The use of Ukrainian and Russian drones was criticised by France and Germany, while the United States pointed out that the Russia-led side has repeatedly violated agreements by the use of drones and howitzer artillery.
title: War in Donbas

text: In December 2021, Ukrainian authorities said that Russia was sending snipers and tanks to the region.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 21 January 2022, the Chairman of the Russian State Duma, Vyacheslav Volodin, called for a discussion in the parliamentary body to recognise the independence of the Donbas region and its separation from Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: There was a sharp increase in artillery shelling by the Russian-led militants in Donbas, which was considered by Ukraine and its allies to be an attempt to provoke the Ukrainian army or create a pretext for invasion.
title: War in Donbas

text: For example, the Ukrainian military reported enduring 60 attacks along the line of contact on 17 February alone, including "one shell that struck a kindergarten near the front line, injuring three staff".
title: War in Donbas

text: Amid increased tensions between Russia and Ukraine in February 2022, Russian president Vladimir Putin announced on 21 February that Russia would recognise the independence of the Donetsk and Luhansk people's republics.
title: War in Donbas

text: This announcement was followed by an order to deploy Russian troops to the Donbas as "peacekeepers".
title: War in Donbas

text: The DPR and LPR joined the offensive; the separatists stated that an operation to capture the entirety of Donetsk Oblast and Luhansk Oblast had begun.
title: War in Donbas

text: By 25 March 2022, Russian forces claimed control over 93 percent of Luhansk oblast and 54 percent of Donetsk oblast.
title: War in Donbas

text: The International Criminal Court judged that the war was both a national and international armed conflict involving Ukraine and Russia, and the European Court of Human Rights judged that Russia controlled the rebels from 2014 onward.
title: War in Donbas

text: Only Russian intervention in August 2014 prevented a Ukrainian victory in the conflict.
title: War in Donbas

text: Before the war, separatism in the Donbas had limited support, and there was little evidence of support for an armed uprising.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russia took advantage of this, however, to launch a co-ordinated political and military campaign against Ukraine, as part of the broader Russo-Ukrainian War, including several information campaigns and sporadic cyber attacks that started before Yanukovych's ouster in February.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russian president Vladimir Putin gave legitimacy to the separatist movement when he described the Donbas as part of the historical "New Russia" (Novorossiya), and said he did not understand how the region ever became part of Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: When Ukrainian authorities cracked down on the pro-Russian protests and arrested local separatist leaders in early March, these were replaced by people with ties to the Russian security services and interests in Russian businesses, probably by order of Russian intelligence.
title: War in Donbas

text: By April 2014, Russians citizens had taken control of the separatist movement, and were supported by volunteers and materiel from Russia, including Chechen and Cossack militants.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to DPR insurgent commander Igor Girkin, without this support in April, the movement would have fizzled out.
title: War in Donbas

text: The First Battle of Donetsk Airport in late May 2014 was the first battle between the separatists and the Ukrainian government that involved large amounts of Russian volunteers.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to the Ukrainian government, at the height of the conflict in the summer of 2014, Russian paramilitaries were reported to make up between 15% and 80% of the combatants.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to the RAND Corporation, "Russia has armed, trained, and led the separatist forces.
title: War in Donbas

text: But even by Kyiv's own estimates, the vast majority of rebel forces consist of locals—not soldiers of the regular Russian military".
title: War in Donbas

text: By August 2014, the Ukrainians had re-taken most of the territory controlled by pro-Russian forces.
title: War in Donbas

text: Igor Girkin urged Russian military intervention, and said that the combat inexperience of his irregular forces, along with recruitment difficulties amongst the local population, had caused the setbacks.
title: War in Donbas

text: He addressed Russian president Vladimir Putin, saying: "Losing this war on the territory that President Vladimir Putin personally named New Russia would threaten the Kremlin's power and, personally, the power of the president".
title: War in Donbas

text: The first sign of this was the 25 August 2014 capture of a group of Russian paratroopers on active service in Ukrainian territory by the Ukrainian security service (SBU).
title: War in Donbas

text: The SBU released photographs of them, and their names.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Russian Defence Ministry said these soldiers had crossed the border "by accident".
title: War in Donbas

text: According to Nikolai Mitrokhin's estimates, by mid-August 2014 during the Battle of Ilovaisk, there were between 20,000 and 25,000 troops fighting in the Donbas on the separatist side, and only 40% to 45% were "locals".
title: War in Donbas

text: Beginning on 27 August 2014, vast amounts of military equipment and troops crossed the border from Russia into southern Donetsk Oblast.
title: War in Donbas

text: Western officials described this as a "stealth invasion".
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian President Poroshenko declared that Russian forces had invaded.
title: War in Donbas

text: Nico Tak estimated on 28 August that "well over" 1,000 Russian soldiers were operating in the Donbas.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukrainian forces were also struck by cross-border shelling from Russia from mid-July onward.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russian officials denied any role, denials viewed as implausible.
title: War in Donbas

text: In the run up to the August 2014 invasion, Russia had decided to replace many of the hardline leaders of the separatist movement, including Igor Girkin and DPR prime minister Alexander Borodai.
title: War in Donbas

text: These replacements, taken together with the subsequent invasion, represented another turning point in the conflict.
title: War in Donbas

text: It abandoned the Russian citizen-led separatist project, which it had been unable to fully control, and replaced it with the idea of special status for Donbas within Ukraine, and a more obedient DPR/LPR command.
title: War in Donbas

text: This represented a Russian attempt to "indigenise" the conflict, using the insignificant local pro-Russian political activists as cover for the advancement of Russian interests in Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russian forces and equipment participated in the Second Battle of Donetsk Airport and the Battle of Debaltseve.
title: War in Donbas

text: A report released by the Royal United Services Institute in March 2015 said that "the presence of large numbers of Russian troops on Ukrainian sovereign territory" had become a "permanent feature" of the war since the August 2014 invasion.
title: War in Donbas

text: The terms of the Minsk agreement were highly favourable to Russia, in that they required Ukraine to grant "special status" to the separatist-held areas, and reintegrate them into Ukraine, similar to the federalisation espoused by pro-Russian protesters in early 2014.
title: War in Donbas

text: This would establish a Russian "strategic hook" within Ukraine that could be used to prevent future integration of that country with the European Union or NATO.
title: War in Donbas

text: Former DPR prime minister Borodai said that about 50,000 Russian citizens had fought for the separatists by mid-2015, not counting regular Russian troops that had invaded.
title: War in Donbas

text: In a press conference on 17 December 2015, Russian president Vladimir Putin acknowledged for the first time that there had been a Russian military presence in the Donbas, though saying this did not mean there were "Russian troops" there.
title: War in Donbas

text: By September 2015, the separatist units, at the battalion level and up, were acting under direct command of officers of the Russian Armed Forces.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukraine, the United States, and some analysts consider them to be under the command of Russia's 8th Combined Arms Army, which was re-formed within the Russian Southern Military District for this specific task in 2017.
title: War in Donbas

text: By February 2018, the number of separatist forces were estimated at 31,000, of which 80% (25,000) were estimated to be Donbas residents, 15% (≈5,000) military contractors from Russia and other countries, and 3% (900–1,000) regular Russian soldiers.
title: War in Donbas

text: On 24 April 2019, Putin issued an executive order fast-tracking the process for obtaining Russian citizenship for residents of the territories held by the DPR and the LPR.
title: War in Donbas

text: This "passportisation" is similar to what Russia did in Transnistria, Abkhazia, and South Ossetia.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russia recognised the DPR and LPR as independent states on 21 February 2022, and subsequently ordered Russian troops into the Donbas conflict zone as "peacekeepers".
title: War in Donbas

text: This was followed by the launch of a full-scale invasion of Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: Initially, these were to be kept away from the front, but after a second delivery of similar weapon systems they were cleared for use anywhere.
title: War in Donbas

text: In September 2021, Kyiv commanded military forces drill in a common exercise with US and NATO partners.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to the Uppsala Conflict Data Program, this casualty marked the dividing line between peace and armed conflict.
title: War in Donbas

text: The threshold to an armed conflict was crossed in the beginning of May when more than 25 people were killed since the beginning of the conflict.
title: War in Donbas

text: By mid-July to early August more than one thousand people died, the minimum threshold for classifying it as a war.
title: War in Donbas

text: The estimated number of fatalities in the Donbas war was 14,200–14,400 by the end of December 2021, including non-combat military deaths.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights, 6,500 were pro-Russian separatist forces, 4,400 were Ukrainian forces, and 3,404 were civilians.
title: War in Donbas

text: The vast majority of civilian deaths were in the first two years of the war, while 365 civilians were killed in the six years from 2016 to 2021.
title: War in Donbas

text: In the year before Russia's full-scale invasion, 25 civilians were killed, over half of them from mines and unexploded ordnance.
title: War in Donbas

text: Of the civilian deaths, at least 312 were foreigners: 298 passengers and crew of Malaysia Airlines Flight 17, 11 Russian journalists, an Italian journalist, a Lithuanian diplomat, and one Russian civilian killed in cross-border shelling.
title: War in Donbas

text: Ukraine claimed 7,577 separatists had been killed and 12,000 were missing by early 2015.
title: War in Donbas

text: They claimed an additional 103 Russian servicemen were killed between January and April 2016.
title: War in Donbas

text: In late August 2015, according to a reported leak by a Russian news site, Business Life (Delovaya Zhizn), 2,000 Russian soldiers had been killed in Ukraine by February 2015.
title: War in Donbas

text: The UN reported growing lawlessness in the region, documenting cases of targeted killings, torture, and abduction, primarily carried out by the forces of the Donetsk People's Republic.
title: War in Donbas

text: The UN also reported threats against, attacks on, and abductions of journalists and international observers, as well as beatings and attacks on supporters of Ukrainian unity.
title: War in Donbas

text: Russia criticised these reports, and said that they were "politically motivated".
title: War in Donbas

text: There were also multiple instances of beatings, abductions, and possible executions of local residents by Ukrainian troops, such as Oleh Lyashko's militia and the Aidar territorial defence battalion.
title: War in Donbas

text: In August, Igor Druz, a senior advisor to pro-Russian insurgent commander Igor Girkin, said that "On several occasions, in a state of emergency, we have carried out executions by shooting to prevent chaos.
title: War in Donbas

text: As a result, our troops, the ones who have pulled out of Sloviansk, are highly disciplined".
title: War in Donbas

text: After the first Minsk Protocol ceasefire, warlords took control of districts on the separatist side.
title: War in Donbas

text: The New York Times reported that the high rate of civilian deaths had "left the population in eastern Ukraine embittered toward Ukraine's pro-Western government", and that this sentiment helped to "spur recruitment" for the insurgents.
title: War in Donbas

text: A March 2016 report by the OHCHR said that people that lived in separatist-controlled areas were experiencing "complete absence of rule of law, reports of arbitrary detention, torture and incommunicado detention, and no access to real redress mechanisms".
title: War in Donbas

text: As consequence of the conflict, large swathes of the Donbas region, on both sides of the "contact line", have become contaminated with landmines and other explosive remnants of war (ERW).
title: War in Donbas

text: According to the UN Humanitarian Coordinator in Ukraine, in 2020 Ukraine was one of the most mine-affected countries in the world, with nearly 1,200 mine/ERW casualties since the beginning of the conflict in 2014.
title: War in Donbas

text: In addition, more than 800,000 Ukrainians had sought asylum, residence permits, or other forms of legal stay in neighbouring countries, with over 659,143 in Russia, 81,100 in Belarus, and thousands more elsewhere.
title: War in Donbas

text: According to another report by the UN OHCHR, over three million people continued to live in the Donbas conflict zone as of March 2016.
title: War in Donbas

text: This was said to include 2.7 million who lived in DPR and LPR-controlled areas, and 200,000 in Ukrainian-controlled areas adjacent to the line of contact.
title: War in Donbas

text: In 2011, Ukrainian Luhansk and Donetsk oblasts had a combined population of 6.1 million.
title: War in Donbas

text: As a result of Russian military aggression in 2014, about 2 million had to leave the region as internally displaced or as refugees.
title: War in Donbas

text: After a full-scale Russian invasion in 2022, another approximately 3 million either fled or were killed, in total resulting in an 80% decrease of the Donbas population.
title: War in Donbas

text: A September 2014 International Republican Institute poll of the Ukrainian public (excluding those in Russian-annexed Crimea) had 89% of respondents opposing Russian military intervention in Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: As broken down by region, 78% of those polled from Eastern Ukraine (including Dnipropetrovsk Oblast) opposed Russian intervention, along with 89% in Southern Ukraine, 93% in Central Ukraine, and 99% in Western Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: As broken down by native language, 79% of Russian speakers and 95% of Ukrainian speakers opposed Russian intervention. 80% of those polled said that Ukraine should remain a unitary country.
title: War in Donbas

text: A poll conducted by the same institute in 2017 showed that 80% of Ukrainians nationally and 73% of people from the Ukrainian-controlled areas of Donbas believed the separatist republics should remain as part of Ukraine.
title: War in Donbas

text: Around 60% of the people polled did not believe Ukraine was doing enough to regain the lost territories because of the Minsk agreements.
title: War in Donbas

text: The Battle of Soledar was a series of military engagements in and around the urban-type settlement of Soledar during the Battle of Donbas in the Russo-Ukrainian war.
title: Battle of Soledar

text: Russian forces began an offensive on 3 August 2022, shelling Soledar, Bakhmut, and surrounding villages to the south and east.
title: Battle of Soledar

text: Repeated local Russian assaults on or near Soledar continued into October, with many repelled by the Ukrainian army.
title: Battle of Soledar

text: By late December, much of the fighting in the region centered around Bakhmut, with Soledar seen as a satellite stronghold protecting the city's northeastern flank and supply lines.
title: Battle of Soledar

text: By this time, much of Soledar had been reduced to ruins.

title: Battle of Soledar

text: The OCHA did not give an exact location of the convoy's destination nor explain how it secured the safety of the convoy, only that it came from Dnipro and that both the Ukrainians and Russians were notified in advance.

text: The Russians wanted to set conditions to disrupt Ukrainian control over the T0513 trunk road that supports Ukrainian frontline positions in northeast Donetsk Oblast, the ISW continued.
title: Battle of Soledar

text: Following the capture of Bakhmutske and lack of momentum in Bakhmut in late December 2022, analysts believed Russia would transfer troops to launch an attack on Soledar in early January 2023.
title: Battle of Soledar

text: On 13 January, Ukrainian Defence Minister Oleksii Reznikov called the fighting "very difficult" and said aggressive tactics by PMC Wagner mercenaries resulted in high casualties.
title: Battle of Soledar

text: Reznikov said he believed Wagner was fighting for control of the mineral wealth offered by the mines in the region.
title: Battle of Soledar

text: Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty correspondent Maryan Kushnir, embedded with a Ukrainian assault battalion during the battle, described the Soledar battle as a "flanking fight" amid freezing temperatures and frozen ground.
title: Battle of Soledar

text: In January 2023, as the Russians advanced into Soledar, analysts began dismissing the strategic value of the settlement, assessing that a Russian victory in Soledar would be pyrrhic at best.
title: Battle of Soledar

text: National Security Council, who supports Ukraine, said on 12 January that "even if both Bakhmut and Soledar fall to the Russians, it's not going to have a strategic impact on the war itself".

text: The Battle of Kyiv was part of the Kyiv offensive in the Russian invasion of Ukraine for control of Kyiv, the capital city of Ukraine, and surrounding districts including Hostomel Airport.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: The combatants were elements of the Russian Armed Forces and Ukrainian Armed Forces.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: The battle lasted from 24 February 2022 to 2 April 2022 and ended with the withdrawal of Russian forces.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Initially, Russian forces captured key areas to the north and west of Kyiv, leading to international predictions of the city's imminent fall.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Poor Russian logistics and tactical decisions helped the defenders to thwart efforts at encirclement, and, after a month of protracted fighting, Ukrainian forces began counterattacking.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Following the successful Ukrainian counterattacks in late March, Russia began withdrawing its forces from the Kyiv area on 29 March.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Street battles followed and the presidential compound was barricaded, while Russian paratroopers made two attempts to storm the compound.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: On the morning of 25 February, three Russian saboteurs, dressed as Ukrainian soldiers, entered Obolonskyi District, 10 kilometres (6 mi) north of the Verkhovna Rada building, the seat of the parliament of Ukraine.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: According to a later claim, the "saboteurs" could in fact have been Ukrainian soldiers from the 72nd Mechanized Brigade, who were mistaken for Russian troops.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Several hours later, a Ukrainian Sukhoi Su-27 fighter aircraft piloted by Colonel Oleksandr Oksanchenko was shot down over the city and crashed into an apartment building.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Throughout the day, gunfire was heard at several areas of the city.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Ukrainian officials described the gunfire as arising from clashes with Russian forces.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: There was heavy gunfire in the city during the night.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Concurrently, Ukrainian forces repelled an attack on a power plant in the northeastern neighborhood of Troieshchyna.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: The attack was suggested to be a Russian attempt to cut off Kyiv's electricity supply.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Heavy fighting occurred near Kyiv Zoo, where Ukrainian forces defended an army base on Prospect Peremohy.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: This accident, as well as a shootout on the nearby junction with Povitroflotskyi Avenue, which took place on the evening of 26 February, were later clarified to have been friendly fire accidents.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: According to Zelenskyy, Ukrainian forces managed to repel a Russian offensive and continued to hold Kyiv and its surrounding areas.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: A curfew was imposed from 17:00 to 08:00, and violators were to be considered to be saboteurs.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: According to the British Ministry of Defence, Russian forces were 31 kilometres (19 mi) from the city center.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: On 27 February, clashes between Ukrainian forces and Russian saboteurs continued, whilst local officials remained adamant that the city was still under full Ukrainian control.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Later that morning, a rocket fell and exploded in the courtyard of a 16-story high rise building in Troieshchyna, destroying several cars.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Ukrainian officials allege that the missile was fired from Belarus.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: By the evening, the Associated Press reported that mayor of Kyiv, Vitali Klitschko had stated the city was encircled, which was subsequently clarified in The Kyiv Independent by his spokesperson as a mistake.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: The report has since been classified as false information.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: During the night, a Russian military convoy attempted to set up a temporary base at Syrets Metro, which was met with a deadly confrontation with Ukrainian troops.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Zelenskyy urged citizens of Kyiv to respond to the Russian assault with improvised attacks using Molotov cocktails.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Residents were warned to avoid windows and balconies. 18,000 guns were distributed amongst citizens during the first day of the battle, while the Ukrainian Territorial Defense Forces, normally kept in reserve, were activated upon the start of the attack.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Satellite images revealed the existence of a long column of Russian vehicles heading to Kyiv along a 64-kilometre-long (40 mi) highway approaching from the north, approximately 39 km (24 mi) from the city center.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Ukrainian soldiers killed an Israeli-Ukrainian citizen at a checkpoint, mistaking him for a Chechen member of the Russian army.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: On the morning of 1 March, the Russian Ministry of Defense issued an emergency evacuation notice to local civilians stating that they intended to target Ukrainian transmission facilities around Kyiv and that all nearby residents should leave.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Hours later, a Russian missile struck the Kyiv TV Tower, killing five people and injuring five others.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: The attack severed all television transmissions in Kyiv.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Meanwhile, the Babi Yar Holocaust Memorial Center confirmed an accidental hit by a second missile intended for the tower on a memorial dedicated for the Babi Yar Massacre.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: A Russian airstrike also struck and damaged an occupied maternity clinic.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Further Russian shelling struck the neighborhoods of Rusanivka and Kurenivka and the suburbs of Boyarka and Vyshneve, as well as the area around Kyiv International Airport.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Later in the morning, Klitschko stated that the Russian army was beginning to surround the city in an attempt to enforce a blockade.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Klitschko told Channel 24 that tanks were approaching Kyiv from Belarus and that Ukrainian authorities were inspecting Ukrainian checkpoints for preparedness.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: The Estonian Defence Forces intelligence chief estimated that the advancing Russian convoy would arrive to Kyiv's outer suburbs in at least two days, after which they would try to lay siege to the city.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Polish president Andrzej Duda said Zelenskyy had told him that Ukrainian forces would not withdraw from Kyiv.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: On the night of 24 February, the city was divided by the Ukrainian armed forces into sectors, with generals assigned to each one, following a chain of command leading up to Syrskyi.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: All of Kyiv Oblast's aviation capabilities had been moved to different locations a week before the invasion.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: The 72nd Mechanized Brigade was the only tank brigade that could defend the capital.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Debris from an intercepted Russian rocket fell on the Kyiv-Pasazhyrskyi railway station, damaging a major heating pipeline.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: The resulting explosion caused minor damage to the station.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: On 3 March, The New York Times estimated that over 15,000 people were sheltering in the city's subway.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: The UK Ministry of Defence stated that over the past three days the advancing Russian convoy had made "little discernable progress" going forward.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: The following day, a wave of shelling struck downtown Kyiv, including the Borshchahivka neighbourhood.

text: A CNN investigation found that the strikes had hit a business center and multi-story buildings in the western areas of the city.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: On 7 March, Ukrainian authorities claimed Ukrainian forces had destroyed two Russian aircraft.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Later, Zelenskyy denied rumors that he had fled the city, insisting that he intended to stay in Kyiv.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: The attempt by the Russians to encircle Kyiv failed.

text: On 10 March, Klitschko stated that nearly two million people, half of the population of Kyiv, had fled the city since the war began.

text: Another fire occurred on Synioozerna Street in the northwestern edge of the city.

text: On the morning of 14 March, a Russian shell struck a 9-story residential building on Bohatyrska Street in Obolon.

text: The building was partially destroyed, with at least one person killed and 12 wounded.

text: Another Russian rocket was shot down over Kyiv, with its fragments damaging a 5-story residential building in Kurenivka, killing one person.

text: In addition, Russian forces fired three rockets at the Antonov Serial Production Plant, injuring seven people.

text: Four people were killed by shelling in Sviatoshynskyi.

text: That day, an EU delegation visited Kyiv and subsequently met with Zelenskyy, who urged other leaders to follow suit.

text: In Sviatoshynskyi, fragments of a missile fell on a residential area.

text: According to local officials, six houses and four schools were damaged; about 200 people had to be evacuated.

text: Ukrainian forces evacuated thousands of people from nearby suburbs and settlements, including 20,000 people in Boryspil.

text: That day, a delegation of Baltic parliament speakers visited Kyiv.

text: A British intelligence report on 25 March said that Ukraine had retaken towns as far as 35 kilometres (22 mi) from the city as Russian forces began to run out of supplies.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: The Russian military claimed it had successfully destroyed the largest major oil terminal in the country, which was close to Kyiv.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Following the successful Ukrainian counterattacks in late March, Russia announced it was withdrawing its forces from the Kyiv area on 29 March.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: Taking Kyiv was deemed to be a key objective and their failure to take it was viewed as setback for the campaign.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: On 1 April, EU parliament president Roberta Metsola visited Kyiv, becoming the first EU top official to travel to Ukraine since the start of the invasion.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: On 2 April, Ukrainian authorities announced that the entire Kyiv region had been recaptured.

text: International media outlets reported jubilation among the civilian population in the capital and other areas from which the Russians retreated.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: The discovery of Russian war crimes committed during the offensive, particularly in Bucha, led to additional rounds of sanctions against Russia and pledges of further military aid to Ukraine.
title: Battle of Kyiv (2022)

text: For international military observers, the retreat was a surprise upset that dispelled notions of a quick Russian victory and showcased Ukraine's resilience, as well as unexpected weaknesses in the Russian military.
